name: Nobottle
description: Nobottle was a small logging village just off the Fairwater road between Little Delving and Bywater. Its Cutters' Guild was known throughout the Westfarthing.

name: Vamag
description: Built in the mid-Second Age.. The rural retreat for Sauron's servant Adûnaphel, reputed to be one of the Nazgûl.. The local Coastal Peoples fear this site, declaring it to be haunted.

name: Undrond
description: Udûdhrond ('Deep-hidden vault') was once a township of 4,300 on the west bank of the Gwathló, about eighty miles downriver from Tharbad. In its early Third Age heyday it was a prosperous inland port. By the mid-Third Age its population had collapsed following the Great Plague and Cardolan's disintegration, and by T.A. 1970 the town was fully abandoned. What remains is a stretch of crumbling stone foundations along the riverbank, half-hidden by vegetation — a haunted silence where a busy market once stood.

name: Ost-in-edhil
description: Founded by Galadriel and Celeborn around S.A. 750.. An Elven residence; smith halls for the magical crafters known as the Gwaith-i-Mírdain.. Celebrimbor the Smith, the forger of the Great Rings, made his home here, as did the fairly disguised Sauron.

name: Tolfalas
description: The island of Tolfalas is a large, remote island guarding the mouth of the Anduin, its geography ranging from rugged coastal cliffs to sheltered inlets. The principal settlement (Gobel Tolfalas) sits at the end of a long firth on the southeastern coast, around a haven that has served as a Gondorian naval base since T.A. 759. Stone houses climb the ridge above the firth; the warden's small keep overlooks the water. The island's isolation and strategic position make it a place of passing sailors and military men more than settled farmers.

name: Ostara
description: A small Gondorian town of southeastern Belfalas, considered part of the County of Eregost. Its stone houses face the inland hills; the town serves as a minor administrative centre and market for the surrounding farmland and small estates.

name: Eregost
description: A harbour-town in southeastern Belfalas and chief settlement of the County of Eregost. Its stone quays face the open waters of the Bay of Belfalas; fishing boats and small trading vessels keep the harbour busy. The town's houses are whitewashed stone with tiled roofs, and the smell of salt and tar is constant.

name: Lindon
description: An Elvish coastal settlement in Lindon, one of the last Elvish domains in the west of Middle-earth. The settlement is small and quiet — elegant timber-and-stone buildings half-hidden among trees near the shore, maintained by Elves who prefer the company of the sea to that of Men. A traveller is struck by the orderliness and beauty of everything here: tools put away neatly, gardens tended with obvious care, and always somewhere in the background the sound of music or quiet voices.

name: Sarlond
description: A small Gondorian town on the Cape of Sarlond in northeastern Belfalas, between Thonfalas and Eregost. Its stone houses and small harbour face the open waters of the bay; fishing and coastal trade are the main activities. The cape gives good shelter from westerly storms.

name: Thonfalas
description: A small coastal town in easternmost Belfalas, on the western part of the Gilrain-Serni bay. Its stone fishermen's houses face the water; the bay provides good anchorage and rich fishing. The town is a quiet, salt-wind-scoured settlement dependent on the sea.

name: Nargond
description: A township of Belfalas on the coastal road, roughly halfway between Ráma and Endil. It is the chief settlement of the county of the same name — a modest Gondorian town of stone houses, fishing boats and small trading vessels. The coastal road keeps it connected to the wider economy of Belfalas.

name: Rama
description: A town of Gondor or the coastal region whose character and economy reflect the local geography.

name: Bar Algund
description: A small town of western Belfalas, lying close to the great seat of Dol Amroth and within the same Grithion. Bar Algund's stone houses and small harbour face the Bay of Belfalas; the town relies on fishing and on trade with the Prince's court nearby. Its name derives from an old Gondorian family that once held the local lands.

name: Caras Amarth
description: Built by King Oropher, circa S.A. 2255.. Former royal palace of the Woodland Realm.. Similar to the Aradhrynd, the palace was a network of caverns hollowed out of a hillside deep within the forest.

name: Goblin-town
description: Natural caverns occupied by Orcs since at least the Second Age.. A base for raiding and dominating the Misty Mountain passes; a breeding ground for Orcs.. Goblin-town is an Orc-infested system of caverns near the High Pass connecting Imladris to the lands of Rhovanion.

name: Emlin
description: A small fishing village in the southernmost Angle of Lossarnach, where the river Erui meets the great Anduin. Its stone houses and wooden wharves face the confluence; the people here are fishermen who work the rich waters where the rivers meet. Flat-bottomed boats and nets are spread on every bank.

name: Bar Erui
description: A mountain hamlet of no more than thirty-five households in the foothills of the Blue Mountains (Ered Luin), known as 'Eruimar' or 'Lonely Home'. Though remote, it is easy enough to spot — travellers lost in the foothills look for its stone houses on the hillside. Founded as a Dúnedain trading post for trappers and mountaineers, it retains that character in the mid-Third Age, with a small Laughing Brook running through the settlement. Many of its roughly 150 inhabitants are Riverman or Northman stock, and the tavern here can fill a long evening with wild tales from the hills.

name: Imloth Melui
description: Imloth Melui ('Sweet flower-valley') is a fertile valley in Lossarnach dotted with small settlements, orchards and herb-gardens. Its principal settlement is Arnach, seat of Forlong the Fat, Lord of Lossarnach. The valley is famous for its roses and healing herbs; a traveller riding through in spring is struck by the colour and fragrance of the terraced slopes. The Hall of the Gentle Hand and the Hall of Lady Vanyalos are known throughout southern Gondor for their healers.

name: Barad Othnir
description: A castle and small settlement in northeastern Harithilien, perched on a rocky spur of the western Ephel Dúath. Its tower overlooks the main road below, offering a commanding view of traffic moving between Ithilien and Mordor. The castle walls are of dark mountain stone; the name means 'Tower of the War-wager' in Sindarin. A small garrison and their families live in the shadow of the keep.

name: Rianbar
description: A town of Harithilien, situated south of the Emyn Arnen along the river Dinnen. Its stone houses and market square serve the southern Ithilien farmlands; the Dinnen valley is known for its olive groves and herb gardens, and the town's small harbour handles river traffic to and from Pelargir.

name: Aludor
description: A small town in southwestern Harithilien, set halfway between Hyarpendë and Tir Ethraid on the road through the region. The town is notable as the seat of House Alagdor, one of Ithilien's ancient noble families, whose home castle Târik-Ûriyat rises on a rocky spur above the surrounding fields and olive groves. Stone houses with tiled roofs line the main road; the air carries the scent of herbs and cultivated terraces.

name: Bar Nepos
description: A small town and watchtower at the mouth of the river Nethos in southwestern Harithilien. Home of the noble house of Nethos, whose tower overlooks the river confluence and the road to Pelargir. The town's houses are compact, stone-built and practical; the watchtower is the tallest structure for miles.

name: Bar Morvegil
description: A small town of Harithilien on a tributary of the Poros, once known as Bar Miruvor ('House of Precious-juice'). It was renamed Bar Morvegil ('House of the Black-sword') when the local noble family took a martial crest. Its stone houses and small river-wharf serve travellers on the Poros road; orchards and herb-gardens cover the gentle slopes around it.

name: Hyarpendë
description: The most important trading town of Harithilien, Hyarpendë ('South Slope' in Quenya) sits on the east bank of the Anduin opposite Pelargir, at the northern end of the Hyarmentie road. A ferry runs every two hours across to Pelargir, usually packed with merchants, horses and goods. The town has grown from a lazy village into a bustling commercial centre: merchants deal in horses and mules, a coach line runs south along the Poros, and the Five Falcons inn feeds the weary (at high prices). At the town centre stands a thirty-foot Númenórean obelisk marking the road's end.

name: Sindamos
description: A lordly manor in western Harithilien, the main settlement of the county of Glorel. Known in Sindarin as Thindamost ('Grey ascending house'), it sits on a gentle slope above the Ithilien farmlands. The manor house is a Gondorian stone hall with outbuildings and enclosed gardens; the surrounding county is known for olives and herbs.

name: Trenas
description: The seat of Tolfalas' royal warden and the island's principal settlement (also known as Gobel Tolfalas). Located at the end of a long southeastern firth, with a haven for warships. Stone houses cluster at the foot of a ridge; the warden's small keep stands on the slope above. The town grew from a military base into a modest civilian settlement during the Third Age, though it remains a place of soldiers and sailors more than merchants.

name: Rond Orro
description: A manor in the region, its character and household determined by its local lord.

name: Tir Bronadui
description: A Noldor Elvish settlement on the northern coast of Lothlann — the only Elvish community in these chill lands. Originally a village with a tall lamp-house guiding lost sea-farers, it grew into a purely military outpost guarding the route around the Ered Luin into Lothlann and Forlindon. Its stone watchtower is visible for miles along the grey coastline; the Noldor here are few, disciplined and watchful. After the destruction of Angmar (T.A. 1975) the garrison was largely lost trying to rescue Arvedui, and the survivors departed south.

name: Sarn Athrad
description: Sarn Athrad ('Stone Ford') is the ancient stony ford of the Dwarves over the river Gelion near the influx of the Rathloriel, across which the old Dwarf Road from the Blue Mountains entered East Beleriand. It was here that the Battle of Sarn Athrad was fought between Elves and Dwarves of Nogrod in the First Age. By the mid-Third Age the old ford has been replaced by Edenathrad a little further north; the original ford is a quiet and wild place, remembered in legend but little-visited.

name: Tol Ramdal
description: A small island east of the Gulf of Lhûn in southern Forlindon, serving as the main harbour for High Elves setting out on sea-journeys to southern lands. The island's small stone quay and beacon-tower are maintained by a handful of Elves; the air here carries the salt of the open sea and the faint music of those about to sail west.

name: Vorondrast
description: A watchtower in Forlindon, maintained as part of the system of watch-posts guarding the coasts and passes of this northwestern Elvish realm. Stone-built and exposed to the elements; a small garrison keeps watch.

name: Amon Ereb
description: A lone hill of gentle but commanding slopes in southern Forlindon, the highest point between the Ramdal and the Gelion. In the Third Age, the tower of Car Sidh ('House of Peace') crowns its summit — seat of Rhiannon, a Mithren noblewoman who patronises the largest house of Eldarin healers in Forlindon. About two hundred Elves dwell here; their halls are bright and ordered, and the terraced slopes bear the faint traces of much older First-Age earthworks and ditches dug in the wars against Morgoth. The hill is a landmark visible for miles across the flat southern lands.

name: Rhomros
description: Rhomros ('Loudfoam') is a town in Galfalas where the Tauron (Celos) river meets the Bay of Lune. Timber cut from the forests of Forlindon is floated downstream here for ship construction; a small shipyard and sawmill dominate the waterfront. Low stone and timber houses face the bay; the smell of resin and sawdust hangs in the air.

name: Mindon Bron
description: A watchtower at Caranthir's Pass in Forlindon, maintained by the Rangers of Lindon to guard the route against intruders coming around the Ered Luin. A small garrison keeps the beacon burning; the tower is a plain stone structure on a windswept ridge with a view of the pass below.

name: Tir Eiliant
description: The lordly seat of Anfalas, traditional home of the provincial governor (Cáno). A stone manor house and associated buildings in a defensible position overlooking Anfalas; the Cáno's household and administrative staff maintain it as the principal seat of authority for the coastal plain.

name: Amon Draug
description: The highest hill in Forlindon, Amon Draug, sat near the mid-point of the Emyn Cûn hills, where the Gelion began. It was possible to see Belegaer from it’s summit.

name: Talanobel
description: A Nando-Sinda village ruled by descendants of the Guest-elves of Arthorien, Ithilbor's line. Its name means 'Flet-town'; buildings are set on platforms in the trees above the rapids of a nearby stream which the villagers use for small industries. Light filters through the canopy and the sound of water is constant.

name: Cebir Luin
description: A steep rocky hill south of the Pinnath Luin in Forlindon, the site of a battle in S.A. 1700 between Dwarves and Elves against a pursuing Orc force. The hill's flanks are honeycombed with old Dwarven excavations. By the Third Age it is a ruin: the ancient earthworks are overgrown, the tunnels dark and dripping, and travellers passing below glance up at the jagged stone crest with unease.

name: Tawarobel
description: The chief settlement of Dor Lelvin, belonging to the manor of Bar Erui at the end of the Ramb Luin. A village of about 150 individuals, mostly mortals with a small Elvish presence; the manor was long associated with Gildor Inglorion's line. Stone and timber houses sit at the edge of an old Elven estate whose gardens and orchards are still tended with Elvish care.

name: Barad Caranthit
description: A fortified hilltop in Forlindon south of the Pinnath Luin, honeycombed with old Dwarven excavations. The site of a battle in S.A. 1700, where a band of Dwarves and Elves made a last stand against pursuing Orcs; though most perished, one escaped to rouse a Dwarven host. The old tunnels and shafts are still there, and locals avoid going too deep. By the Third Age the hilltop is a ruin — crumbling walls and overgrown earthworks visited only by the bold or the curious.

name: Luithen
description: A village in Forlindon or the Blue Mountains area, small and quiet; details of its inhabitants and economy are not well documented in current records.

name: Gwaerost
description: A castle in southwestern Anfalas held by House Maldring of Haerlond. Its stone towers overlook the coastal road; the surrounding estate grows grain and raises sheep on the wind-swept Anfalas plain. The castle is a working garrison and manor combined — practical rather than elegant, with a small garrison and the lord's household sharing its walls.

name: Rond Deren
description: A lordly manor tucked in the hills of northern Anfalas between Umb Estelwain and Rond Rhandir, in the County of Agarinna. Rond Deren is a stone manor house with attached farmsteads — a quiet, well-maintained estate of a minor Gondorian noble family who tend their lands and keep out of larger politics.

name: Stock
description: Settled during Hobbit migrations to the Shire, 1601-1610.. Agriculture.. The fertile soil of the Baranduin River valley produces some of the Shire's best mushrooms.

name: Sunthra Unsar
description: Originally built under the name Mistalonde, the city appeared in records from the mid-Second Age until T.A. 1503, when Gordaig of Saerlann captured and renamed it Sunthra Unsar. The rebuilt city became the seat of power of the Saerlannish (Dynion) clan, and its inhabitants were almost entirely hillmen.

name: Tir Elena
description: A small Gondorian town of Anórien, on the northeastern eaves of Drúadan Forest — capital and seat of House Elenion, the local barons. Stone houses with tiled roofs line the road through the forest's edge; the Drúadan Forest is just beyond the tree-line to the south. The Woses of the forest are never seen in town but their presence is always felt.

name: Bar Nevedui
description: A modest castle of southern Andrast on the river Nevduin, part of the remote county of Bar Morthil. Its walls are of local grey stone, maintained by a small household garrison; the surrounding land is sparsely farmed by Dunir dependants who supply the fort in exchange for protection against Corsair raiders from the coast.

name: Erädas
description: Built by Eärnil I of Gondor around T.A. 934, seized by Umbar in T.A. 1449.. A rural retreat for Lord Teldûmeir of Umbar; the seat of local administration.. Erädas is situated on a sheltered cove in the Bay of Umbar.
inhabitants: 50% Coastal People, 20% Haradan, 20% Corsair, 10% Black Númenórean

name: Isigir
description: Constructed by the Captains of Umbar c. T.A. 320.. A rural power base for Lord Adûmir of Umbar; administration of surrounding farmlands.. Isigir is on the north shore of the Nen Umbar and produces many of the potent herbs for which Umbar is known.
inhabitants: 60% Coastal People, 25% Corsair, 10% Haradan, 5% Black Númenórean

name: Marös
description: Built by Númenórean colonists about S.A. 2210.. Surveillance of traffic on the Nen Umbar; the rural stronghold of Lord Borathôr of Umbar.. Marös rests on a promontory which juts out into the Bay of Umbar.
inhabitants: 30% Coastal People, 30% Corsair, 30% Haradan, 10% Black Númenórean

name: Michel Delving
description: Settled during the Hobbit migrations to the Shire in T.A. 1601-1610.. Largest town in and administrative center of the Shire; agriculture.. The town rests on the slopes of the White Downs in the Westfarthing and is the home of Marcho and Blanco Fallohide.

name: Gwahilmoth
description: A township of Dol Caladir in Minhiriath, about sixty miles west of Tharbad in the central Baranduinean lowlands. In its prime (T.A. 250) it held some 5,000 souls. However by the mid-Third Age its population had dwindled sharply following the Great Plague and regional instability, and the settlement is largely abandoned by T.A. 1640. A traveller finds a melancholy landscape of empty stone foundations and overgrown lanes where grass pushes through the old cobbles.

name: Dol Ninden
description: Dol Nendin ('Hill of Silent Water') is a settlement in Minhiriath that has changed hands in regional conflicts among the Lords Allied and the Cardolandrim. The town sits on a prominent hill above a quiet tributary, its stone walls bearing the scars of past assaults. In peaceful times it is a modest market centre for the surrounding farmland.

name: Entamundo
description: A town in the region, its character shaped by local geography and economy.

name: Cithiel
description: Once a great township of 4,400 on the Baranduin in the Girithlin highlands of Minhiriath, known in its heyday as Cardhath Cirthiel. By T.A. 1400 the old town was abandoned after a long decline. The site has since been resettled on a smaller scale by Eriadorian tribesmen, who call it Cuncan — a rough cluster of timber-and-daub buildings amid the foundations of the much larger earlier settlement.

name: Silent Castle
description: Bar Airlossiel ('Home of the Lonely Snow-maiden'), also known as the Silent Castle, is an ancient Elvish tower in a forgotten corner of southern Sîrgala. Its original community of Silvan Elves and Arthadan refugees pledged fealty to a half-Elven mage, Airlossiel the Quiet, who transformed into one of the Houseless after a morgul-wound in T.A. 1409. Her spirit remains bound to the tower's foundations; the haven became almost eerily peaceful thereafter, and few visitors leave with a full account of what they experienced. Rangers occasionally send incurably wounded men here; some return years later, cured but confused.

name: Parth Ainatir
description: Parth Aertirith (S."Sward of the Holy Watch") was an Elvish camping glade on the border of Sîrgala and Harlindon.The Place was forsaken by the Elves after a great tragedy, the juvenile and drunk Elcaldil of Girithlin, heir of Girithlin, had harassed an elven girl and was thrown down by Glorfindel Goldenhair, after which the House Girithlin thought of it's family and fate as cursed.

name: Endrist
description: Once a populous township of 4,600 in western Siragale, known in its heyday as Enedhriss. By the early Third Age it was already in decline; by T.A. 1400 it had been fully abandoned. A traveller today finds only the ghostly outlines of old foundations under the scrub of the Pinnath Luin's lower slopes — no fire, no voice, only the wind through broken stone.

name: Bag end
description: Built by Bungo Baggins into the Hill above Hobbiton's Bagshot Row as a wedding gift for his wife Belladonna Took, c. T.A. 2890. The most luxurious Hobbit-hole in the Westfarthing, entered through a perfectly round green door with a bright brass knob. Inside, all rooms are on a single level: bedrooms, kitchens, pantries, studies, wardrobes and dining-rooms arranged along a comfortable tunnel-passage. The best rooms face the garden and meadow to the west. Home of Bilbo Baggins and later his heir Frodo Baggins.

name: Aradhrynd
description: Built by Prince Thranduil around T.A. 1050.. Begun as a retreat for the Prince of the Woodland-realm, the halls became a Palace when Thranduil abandoned the halls at Caras Amarth.. The capital and largest city of the Elven kingdom of northern Mirkwood, fashioned after the model of Nargothrond. Using a hill by the edge of the forest as his base, King Thranduil ordered his subjects to carve fair halls and caverns beneath it. Thranduil completed his dwelling in T.A. 1100 after the Shadow of Dol Guldur began growing in the southern reaches of the forest. The halls are not dark, like those of Goblins, but sparkle with torchlight and merriment. Only a small number of Elves actually make their home at the Aradhrynd since most prefer to dwell under the canopy of Mirkwood's leaves.

name: Cerin Amroth
description: Designed by King Amroth of Lórien in S.A. 3447.. Royal palace of Lothlórien.. Cerin Amroth is a grass-covered hill surrounded by two concentric rings of trees.

name: Tir Limlight
description: Settled by Gondorian merchants, circa T.A. 1510.. Trading with Northmen on the River Anduin; a watch-post for potential threats to Gondor from Rhovanion.. Tir Limlight marks the effective northern limit of Gondorian authority and it guards one of the few crossings of the Anduin at the Undeeps.

name: Ost Galad
description: An Elvish settlement in the Emyn Lûm area of Mirkwood, known as Ost Galadh ('Tree Fortress'). In the mid-Third Age its population is about 1,200. The settlement is built among the great forest trees, with platforms and walkways of Silvan Elvish craft connecting the living quarters above the ground. The trees here are ancient and vast; a traveller arriving from outside Mirkwood is struck by the quality of the light filtering through the canopy and the utter silence of the undergrowth.

name: Fornost Erain
description: First fortified by Númenóreans in S.A. 3177, capital of Arthedain after T.A. 1409.. Center of political power in Arthedain; royal seat; intellectual and commercial center of the Dúnedain of the North.. Located on a steep rise on the southern slopes of the North Downs, Fornost is as much a fortress as it is a city.

name: Doldorth
description: A village in Gondor's Ithilien region, a modest settlement of stone farmhouses surrounded by the herb-gardens and orchards for which Ithilien is famed. The traveller arriving here in spring or summer is struck by the fragrance of flowering herbs and the orderly terraces climbing the hillside. Life is quiet; the people grow herbs, olives and grain.

name: Dun Fidach
description: What was once a Dúnadan fortress called Amon Mîn ('Hill between the gaps') — a watch-post on a hill in Dor Wathui commanding the Greenway — has long since been abandoned. The Rangers of the North maintained it briefly after Arnor's dissolution, but eventually left it to decay. Today the Dunlendings call the ruins 'Dinas Gwydd' and a small campsite of Rangers occasionally shelters here, the only visitors to an otherwise forgotten hilltop.

name: Dun Udrost
description: Echad Dagoras ('Dagoras' Camp') is an old Elvish ruin south of Eregion, some sixty miles southeast of Ost-in-Edhil. The Rangers of the North use it as a temporary camp; the local Dunlendings call it Dinas Yddryst or Dol Du ('Dark toll') and shun the place. The ruins are a few broken stone walls and foundation courses amid scrub — impressive in outline, eerie in atmosphere.

name: Creatrach
description: In the Coit Cerdd on the borders of Dunfearn stands the ruin of an old Gondorian watchtower called Harndirion ('Southwatch'), built to keep an eye on the Gwathuirim. The Rangers of the North maintain a semi-permanent camp here among the ancient stones. The local Dunlendings call it Craig-y-Din ('Castle Rock') and give it a wide berth.

name: Gwâl Draig
description: A Dunlending village in Dunfearn that grew up around an ancient Temple of Justice, built c. T.A. 1200. The original temple — shaped like a great stone bull's head, sixty feet tall — now forms the core of a village called Gwâl Draig, home of the Draig-lûth tribe, descendants of the temple's original adherents. The tribe still bears the Dragon Turukulon as their symbol. The village is a rough settlement of timber houses around the old stone temple; guards in white robes and armed with wide-bladed axes watch the entrance.

name: Daithen
description: A Dunnish clan-hold in Dunfearn near the Misty Mountains, its name meaning 'Ancient Fire' in the Dunnish tongue. Daithen is a rough highland village of timber longhouses and livestock pens, its chieftain Menu ruling through a combination of authority and the local conjurer Selyf Hen's half-feared counsel. Smoke from fires hangs low in the valley on cold mornings.

name: Carnach
description: Ost-Dunhoth was once a township of some 3,200 people in Enedhwaith in the early Third Age. By T.A. 1400 its population had drained away entirely. What remains is a field of overgrown ruins — broken walls of old stone half-buried in scrub and grass, with no permanent inhabitants. Local Dunlendings call the place cursed and do not build here.

name: Maenthirost
description: A town in the Shire or northern Eriador whose character reflects the modest farming communities of the region.

name: Witeburg
description: A small Rohirric settlement near the Hornburg, in the Deeping Coomb about four miles beyond Helm's Deep and over a mile northwest of the Juggler's Hall. Its name, 'Castle of Torment', comes from the oddly shaped hill on which it sits — the Witeberg. Wooden walls and timber longhouses; the men here are farmers and fighters in the Rohirric style, big-boned and hardy, their hall hung with weapons and horse-gear.

name: Calost
description: A village of Anórien straddling the Calenen stream on the Great North Road, about thirty miles from Calenhad and twelve from Min-Rimmon. The ancient stone bridge over the Calenen is still in excellent repair. The settlement is typical of the region: stone houses on the western bank cluster around the Traveller's Rest Inn, a well-regarded stopping point for anyone on the road between Minas Anor and Rohan. A mill draws from the river; Fallor's farm on both banks grows barley and wheat. Market days bring peasants from surrounding villages.

name: Astirian
description: The ancestral manor of House Astorion, a noble Gondorian family of Anórien, situated on the fringes of the Mouths of the Entwash north of Min-Rimmon. The manor is a solid Gondorian country house of dressed stone, surrounded by extensive farmlands and pastures. The Astorion family has held these lands since the early Third Age; their hall has witnessed the passage of many royal armies marching the Great North Road nearby.

name: Bar Duilin
description: A town of Anórien situated on the Anduin opposite the island fortress of Cair Andros. Its location makes it a natural waystation for soldiers, messengers and supplies crossing to and from the fortress; a garrison camp and a river-landing dominate the waterfront. Stone houses and wooden barracks line the main road leading to the ferry.

name: Bar-i-Dinuviel
description: Bar-i-Dinuviel (S. Home of the Nightingale; Os. Mbar Tinduvela) was a fishing village on the coast of Dor Tinuviel.

name: Lomost
description: Along with its sister fortress of Aerost, Lómost ('Echo Fortress') is one of two critical coastal beacons defending Anfalas against Corsair raids. Built in T.A. 1634 after the Great Raid on Pelargir, it stands on the western Anfalas coast and can relay news of approaching enemies to Lond Galen or Annon Drúin within hours via its beacon tower. The fortress is a solid coastal structure of dark stone, battered by sea-wind; the garrison here is small but alert.

name: Galenros
description: A tiny Elvish hamlet south of the spur of the Forered Luin — fifteen households of 41 Elves in total, a mixture of various kindreds. It was founded in the Second Age as a communal retreat by High Elven philosophers, artists and nature-lovers who sought refuge from the bustle of Mithlond. The settlement answers to Tawarobel for governance. A visitor finds a handful of delicate timber-and-stone houses half-hidden among the trees, connected by winding paths — an air of quietude and careful order marks everything here.

name: Lond Rhaen
description: A small fishing village on the northwestern coast of Andrast, sheltered in a safe bay of Rast Rhaen. A handful of stone-walled houses face the water; the people here are Dunir fisher-folk whose boats rarely venture far from the bay's protection. The village is quiet and self-sufficient, unknown to most travellers.

name: Bareketta
description: First built in T.A. 407, later rebuilt and expanded.. A rural retreat for the Eketta family; control of the surrounding countryside.. On the eastern shore of Nenuial in Arthedain, Bareketta is the primary estate of the Eketta family, one of the two strongest noble clans in Arthedain. Bareketta was built with beauty and leisure in mind, though it has the strength to withstand a determined assault.

name: Brandy Hall
description: The traditional Great Smial of the patriarch of the Brandybuck clan and Master of Buckland. Built by Gorhendad Oldbuck around T.A. 2340 after founding Buckland, and greatly expanded over generations by his descendants — most notably Gormadoc 'Deepdelver' Brandybuck c. T.A. 2800. This vast complex of tunnels and rooms is delved entirely into Buck Hill, with three large front-doors, many side-doors, and about a hundred windows. The ancestral home of the Brandybuck family; childhood home of Frodo Baggins. Inhabitants: 100% Stoor Hobbit.

name: Dim Lamedon
description: A town of Lamedon in Gondor, lying in the valley of the Lamedon river below steep hills. The name 'Dim Lamedon' references the shadowed character of the valley; the settlement is a modest Gondorian market town of stone houses with slate roofs, serving the scattered farms and pastures of the surrounding uplands.

name: Tumladen
description: A town of Gondor in the Lamedon or Lebennin region, a modest Gondorian settlement of stone houses serving the surrounding farming communities.

name: Celebannon
description: Settled around T.A. 100.. A center for contact between the Elves of Mirkwood and the other folk of Rhovanion.. Celebannon is a small yet elegant Elvish village on the banks of the Taurduin.

name: Bar Gilraen
description: A small town of Lebennin on the lower Gilrain, several leagues northeast of Linhir. It is the seat of the noble family of Bar Gilraen, who hold the surrounding county. Neat stone houses with flat or tiled roofs face a central square; the Gilrain's fertile banks support grain-growing and some wool trade.

name: Diniath
description: A village in the County of Gilrain in southwestern Lebennin, the main settlement of the small barony of the same name. Stone houses and a small market square line the road; the surrounding farmland and fishing on the Gilrain tributary support a modest population.

name: Paths Of The Dead
description: Used as a burial ground by the Daen since the mid-Second Age, last interment in T.A. 679.. Crypts for the royalty of the Daen Coentis.. The Paths of the Dead were originally natural caverns beneath the White Mountains which connected the vale of Dunharrow to the Morthond valley.

name: Lalornen
description: A town of Lebennin on the middle Serni, halfway between Brethil and Linhir. A modest Gondorian market town of stone houses, it serves the farming communities of the Serni valley and acts as a waystation on the road between the two larger settlements.

name: Dol Calimir
description: A town of Lebennin on a small tributary of the Serni, halfway between Minas Brethil and Pelargir. Its position on this route makes it a regular stopping point for travellers moving goods between the interior and Pelargir. Stone houses and a small inn; in recent years the town has been fortified with a new outer wall.

name: Barfalas
description: A small fishing village in Lebennin on the Anduin, southwest of Pelargir. Stone houses sit along the riverbank; the people here are fishermen and small traders who send their catch upriver to Pelargir. The name means 'Home of the Waves' in Sindarin.

name: Arnbad
description: A small town in eastern Methed Lebennin, seat of the surrounding Grithion of the same name. Its name — 'Royal Path' in Sindarin — hints at an old road that once ran through its centre. Stone houses and a modest market hall ring a crossroads shaded by plane trees; the surrounding farmland yields grain and olives.

name: Pellas
description: A small Gondorian town or village in eastern Lebennin, about twenty-five miles northeast of Pelargir in the County of Landor. Also known as Barath Tâlhir, it is a freehold estate of House Tâlhir, a local knightly family. Stone houses and a modest manor; grain and livestock are the main produce of the estate.

name: Minas Airnach
description: A town of western Lebennin in Gondor, whose stone houses and small market serve the farming communities of the surrounding river valley.

name: Mulkan
description: Settled in T.A. 1637 as a result of periodic migrations of Lossoth.. A base for hunting and gathering in the wastes of Forochel.. In winter, hide tents give way to houses built of snow and ice blocks.

name: Bar Luindol
description: A small town of Lossarnach, situated along a tributary of the Erui a few leagues south of Minas Arthoron on the Rathon Gondor. It is the ancestral seat of the noble family of Bar Luindol, who control the surrounding county. Its stone houses and cultivated terraces benefit from the rich volcanic soil of Lossarnach.

name: Echorost
description: A fortified village of southwestern Lossarnach at the confluence of the Celos and the Sirith, the chief settlement of the county of Enedhîr. Its name — 'Ring-fort' — reflects its circular defensive layout: stone walls and a central square that serves as market and muster ground. The surrounding farmland of Lossarnach is rich; the village exports grain, wool and the famous roses of the valley.

name: Nansret
description: Unknown, probably not more than a century old.. A base for hunting and gathering operations.. Nansret is the only known village of the Beffraen, the Wild Men of the Rast Vorn.

name: Barad Dur
description: Raised by Sauron, circa S.A. 1000.. Domination of the plateau of Gorgoroth.. Built on a spur of the Ered Lithui, and fashioned by the power of Sauron's Ruling Ring, Barad-dûr consisted of a great central tower with nine radial towers connected to it by fortified bridges. No stronger fortress ever existed in Middle-earth. Once the seat of Sauron's power, Barad-dûr fell when Isildur cut the Ring from Sauron's hand. Orcs and Trolls now control the area, and no Man has visited Barad-dûr since the Plague.

name: Romenost
description: Built by the Gondorian Eastern Army around T.A. 1399.. A waystation along the Men Romen; a defensive point against invasions from the East.. Romenost serves as a key outpost on the eastern frontier of Gondor.

name: Cirith Ungol
description: Built on the orders of King Isildur, T.A. 2.. Surveillance of the Morgai and the Plateau of Gorgoroth from a vantage point high in the Ephel Dúath.. Despite the tower's many functions, King Tarondor is under pressure to withdraw troops from Cirith Ungol.

name: Hyarlindunt
description: A town in northern Forlindon, its position and character shaped by the cold winds of the northern coast and the sparse population of these lands.

name: Burh Beorills
description: The seat of the Berilas clan along the Men Rómen, halfway between Castle Thorontir and Gadrauhtsbaurg in the hills of Duil Rewinion — known in Rohirric as 'Berilabaurg' or 'Little-bears' Town'. A stockaded Northman settlement of timber halls and longhouses, its cattle-pens and storehouses sit behind a wooden palisade. The Berilas are tough hill-folk; their hall is hung with bear-skins and old weapons.

name: Tir Serni
description: A town and castle of Lebennin on the lower Serni, about 45 miles east of Linhir. Its position on the Serni makes it a waystation for river traffic; the castle overlooks the water from a low promontory. The town's stone houses and small market serve the Serni valley farmers.

name: Ostigrurth
description: Built by Sauron in the mid-Second Age.. Control of traffic in the Den Nurn; the manufacture of finished products; small-scale trade.. Ostigurth is situated at the head of a small canyon on the southern edge of Gorgoroth.

name: Cirith Gorgor
description: Cirith Gorgor (S. "Haunted Pass") was a high pass where the mountain ranges of the Ered Lithui and Ephel Dúath met in the far north-western corner of Mordor. The pass opened out into the mountain vale of Udûn, and was guarded from the beginning of the Third Age by the Towers of the Teeth. When Sauron returned to Mordor c. TA 2951 the Morannon was rebuilt across the Pass.

name: Núrumurl
description: A town in the Mordor borderlands or Nurn region, its character shaped by proximity to the dark power of that land. Life here is hard and closely controlled.

name: Barad Formen
description: A tower of the northern Shire or Arthedain region, its function as a beacon or garrison post defining its character. A plain stone structure on elevated ground.

name: Athrad Poros
description: The ford settlement of Athrad Poros was an important stop on the land trade route south. It began as a military garrison protecting the border between the nascent Kingdom-in-Exile and the Umbarean sphere of influence. When the Harnen Corridor opened, Athrad Poros became a critical juncture for trade with Near Harad and Khand.

name: Barad Wath
description: Gondor began construction on the tower after the fall of Sauron, but abandoned it to conserve resources.. Monitoring traffic between Nurn and Gorgoroth.. The tower rests high in the Eregwath, providing a view for many leagues in many directions.

name: Car Gaul
description: A freehold estate in the County of Nan Lefnui, on the lower Lefnui. It is the property of the Bannor family, who farm the surrounding fields and pastures of this remote corner of Gondor's western frontier. The manor house is a solid stone farmstead with a small courtyard and stableyard.

name: Cair Andros
description: Cair Andros (S. Ship of Long Foam) was an island fortress on the River Anduin, roughly forty miles north of Osgiliath. Shaped like a great ship with a high rocky prow pointing north, it was fortified by Steward Túrin II to protect Anórien from enemy crossings. It served as a vital northern bulwark of Gondor against Mordor throughout the late Third Age.

name: Tir Feren
description: A Gondorian castle overlooking Parth Feren in the westernmost Pinnath Gelin. Built of local stone, it commands the beechwood hills that give it its name ('Beech-ward'). A small garrison and the lord's household maintain the castle; the surrounding parkland of old beeches is peaceful in summer but exposed to the sea-wind in winter.

name: Newbury
description: A Buckland village, the only one set inland from the Brandywine. Before the completion of the High Hay, it occupied a fortified hilltop; the old tower has since been taken down. Today Newbury is a comfortable Hobbit settlement centred on Gorbuck Hall, delved into the hillside. The Hall keeps some almost-forgotten rooms as a Mathom House for relics of the wars against the Old Forest — old weapons, flame-arrows, and mouldy tomes. One or two Gorbucks in every generation study these things quietly, just in case.

name: Eredimar
description: Eredimar (S. Mountain Home) lay at the foot of the Ered Luin within the valley of Imladorn (S. The Wooded Valley). Despite housing fewer than 100 people, it was remarkable for its cultural diversity — Hobbits, Dwarves, Elves, and Men had all settled here at various times. Sulfur springs nearby gave Eredimar renown as a place of healing, and three well-maintained lodges welcomed travelers.

name: Filimer
description: Ruined city.

name: Bar Irlossiel
description: Ruined castle. Barad Perinluoss, commonly known as the Silent Castle, was an ancient Elven refuge in southern Sîrgala — an independent community of Wood-Elves and Arthadan religious refugees who pledged fealty to a half-Elf known as Iralussiel the Tranquil. Iralussiel died mysteriously around the 11th or 12th century T.A., and the shelter became eerily peaceful thereafter.

name: Nan i Naugrim
description: The greatest mine of Nan-i-Naugrim and the capital of the Broadbeam Dwarves since the Second Age. By the mid-Third Age, Edennogrod — the largely ruined Khazad above Nan-i-Naugrim — stood as a shadow of its former self. At the end of the Third Age it was ruled by the feudal lord Grár, who held the honorary title Mountain King among the Shire-folk.

name: Amrûnaur
description: Established circa S.A. 3420 by Prince Edhelion.. Security for the pass through central Dor-en-Ernil; control of the local Dunnish population.. The castle rises from a rocky shelf projecting from the mountain named Thoredarb by the Eredrim. Strategically placed lightning rods protect the fortress during the thunderstorms that frequent the pass.

name: Bar Lithyrn
description: Founded by Gondorian commercial interests in T.A. 1590.. Extraction of mineral ores from northern Ered Lithui.. Most of the inhabitants are local Donaen lured to the mountains by the prospect of wealth. Their Dúnadan lords mine silver here for Gondor's treasuries. The town suffers from isolation, and the population shrinks each year as the silver veins dry up.

name: Dunmardo
description: A small Gondorian town or large village in central Anórien, seat of the county and ancestral holding of the noble family of the same name. Its Sindarin name means 'West-settlement'. Stone houses, a manor and a small market characterise this orderly provincial settlement.

name: Bar Adorn
description: Bar Adorn was a settlement on the River Adorn near the western borders of Rohan, serving as a minor trading post between the Rohirrim and the Dunlendings of the region.

name: Saerthondost
description: Saerthondost was one of two Dúnedain strongholds protecting Pinnath Gelin and Nan Lefnui from the wilds of Hairaverkien. Located on the north bank of the Lefnui where the river turned sharply northwest, it was named for the nearby swamps rich in shepherd's herbs. A lighthouse tower linked it to its sister fortress Pinnornost to the northeast; it was founded in T.A. 823 and abandoned in T.A. 2157.

name: Adavalinda
description: A small village on the eastern bank of the Lefnui, where the river meets the Rámanen confluence. It serves as a staging point for travellers seeking to cross the White Mountains via the Cirith Iaur, some forty miles to the northwest. A ferry here is the last easy crossing before the river narrows to flat-bottomed-boat country upstream. Stone and timber houses cluster around the landing, their occupants largely dependent on guiding travellers and working the river.

name: Thingastathbaurg
description: A village of the Woodmen in the Upper Anduin Vales, the name meaning 'Assembly-Place Fort' in Rhovanion. Its timber longhouses and stockaded hall sit in a forest clearing; the Woodmen here are sturdy folk who live by hunting, forestry and small farming. The great trees of Mirkwood are close by; the village is self-reliant and watchful.

name: Dol Guldur
description: Delved by Dwarves until the middle of the Second Age, occupied by the Necromancer around the year T.A. 1050.. Defense against enemies of the Necromancer; a breeding ground for Orcs and Trolls.. Amon Lanc is an extinct cinder cone in southwestern Mirkwood.

name: Creb Durga
description: Claimed by the Warlord in T.A. 1637.. A strong and secret base of operations in central Cardolan for the raids of the Warlord.. The Warlord's Trolls carved their base out of a craggy hill about seventy miles north of Sudúri.

name: Whitwell
description: A small Hobbit village near Tuckborough in the Shire, known for its good wells and its quiet lanes. Taken over by the Tooks around T.A. 1620 and briefly notorious when residents started charging road tolls and detained a King's Messenger — a stiff fine settled the matter. Paladin Took owns farmland here. The village is small and amiable, though Tooks from Whitwell are reportedly not always welcome at certain fords since the toll incident.

name: Aglarond
description: First used as a refuge by Númenóreans c. S.A. 3331.. Refuge and storehouse for the garrison at Ostiras.. A system of extensive caverns in the northern slopes of the White Mountains. An untapped wealth of gems here serves as an emergency financial reserve for Gondor's Kings.

name: Dol Amroth
description: The name given Lond Ernil after the death of King Amroth of Lórien in T.A. 1981.. Seat for the Prince of Dor-en-Ernil; harbors and building yards for Gondor's navy.. The name given Lond Ernil after the death of King Amroth of Lórien in T.A. 1981.

name: Moria
description: Founded by Durin the Deathless in the First Age.. Exploitation of mineral resources in the central Hithaeglir.. Moria is a Dwarven city delved beneath three peaks in the central Misty Mountains.

name: Frogmorton
description: Settled during migration to Shire, T.A. 1601-1610.. A market town for local farmers.. The town's central location on the Great East Road makes it an ideal spot for regional gatherings.

name: Beldwin
description: First settled by the Dúnedain, circa T.A. 750.. A stable base of administration in the northern Westmarch.. Beldwin lies just south of the River Isen, near an ancient, still used, bridge built by the Dúnedain in days of old.

name: Cillien
description: Settled during Dunnish migrations, T.A. 50-300.. A regional focal point for the scattered Dunnish families of Clan Eogbann; a center for healing.. Cillien is home to a Healer's Hall that serves all of the people in the region.

name: Kuska
description: Founded by Angmar soon after T.A. 1300.. Provisioning the fortress at Morkai.. Kuska is located in the Angmar Gap three miles west of Barad Morkai.
inhabitants: 100% Angmarean (Mixed Mannish: Dunnish, Brygath Easterling, Estaravë)

name: Minas Girithlin
description: Built by Lord of Girithlin around T.A. 590.. Ancestral home of the Princes of Dol Girithlin; domination of the surrounding countryside.. The castle is sturdy and has withstood many a siege.

name: Culwic
description: Culwic, originally known as Himtil or Himobel (S. Chill Village) and later as Colwich, was a small town in western Arthedain on the Little Lune, just north of the borders of Dor Lelvin and Lindon. Its mixed population of Men and Dwarves supported the dwarven settlements to the north through industry and agriculture.

name: Vidusbaurg
description: Buhr Widu, situated near the small Forest Lake, retained some of the royal splendor of the days of Vidugavia. The Grammuz princes who ruled it after the Plague lacked the strength to restore its former glory. Like most Northman villages on the plains, it consisted of long wooden and thatched houses defended by an earthen wall and sturdy wooden towers.

name: Morkai
description: Built by the Witch-king soon after T.A. 1300.. Monitoring post for traffic entering and leaving the Nan Angmar; a base for raids against Arthedain.. Barad Morkai rises from the top of a granite bluff looming over the Angmar Road.

name: Minas Thonfalon
description: Constructed on orders of King Tarcil of Arnor, T.A. 503.. Surveillance of traffic along the coast of the Rast Vorn.. The ruin is located amidst the 'Bog Coast,' and its foundations are slowly sinking in the waterlogged ground.

name: Bar Morthil
description: A royal estate at the westernmost tip of Andrast — the farthest habitation in all of Gondor. A single stone manor overlooks the sheer sea-cliffs of Ras Morthil, where the ocean crashes hundreds of feet below. The estate is maintained year-round by a warden and servants; it faces west toward the open sea, built by the sons of Elendil as a memorial to drowned Númenór. Kings and Stewards sometimes come here for solitude. The only approach is by land along the Men Falas.

name: Amrûnaur
description: Established circa S.A. 3420 by Prince Edhelion.. Security for the pass through central Dor-en-Ernil; control of the local Dunnish population.. The castle rises from a rocky shelf projecting from the mountain named Thoredarb by the Eredrim. Strategically placed lightning rods protect the fortress during the thunderstorms that frequent the pass.

name: Azanulinbar-dûm
description: First delved around T.A. 1000.. The exploitation of mineral wealth in the Emyn Angren.. Azanulinbar-dûm is the principal mansion of the Dwarves of the Iron Hills and the seat of Lord Fulla III. The halls of Azan serve as the homes, workshops, and mines of the Dwarves of Rhovanion. Few outsiders have seen the interior, which is rumored to be a stern beautiful place full of crystals carved by both Naugrim and water. Azan is but a splinter group of a distant House of Naugrim in the East about which little is known.

name: Guinnion
description: Ruined village.

name: Bodroth
description: Bodroth was a city of Eketyar in Arthedain, situated on the northern shores of Lake Evendim about forty miles north of Annúminas. It had a population of roughly 4,900 in T.A. 250, but declined through the Third Age and was eventually abandoned, though it survived the fall of the last King by several generations.

name: Cirith Dudhrandir
description: Cirith Dudhrandir was the only major mountain pass through the Belfalas Peninsula and the only one with a paved road, making it strategically vital for Gondor's southern defenses. At 5,800 feet and over sixty miles east to west, it was guarded by the fortress of Naur Amrûn. The pass was named in S.A. 3400 when Edhelion of Eldalonde received Belfalas as a fief.

name: Cathiel
description: Ruined city. Cathiel was a settlement of Ethir Gwathló in Cardolan, about seventy-five miles inland from the mouths of the Gwathló. It had a population of roughly 4,000 in T.A. 250, declining through the Third Age and finally abandoned by T.A. 1640.

name: Haerlond
description: A small harbour village in Andrast, its modest dock serving ships damaged rounding the dangerous cape. Beneath the village's timber houses lie the buried ruins of an older Númenórean settlement, visible when the ground shifts. Sailors know Haerlond as a last refuge before the open sea; the villagers are skilled at patching hulls and provisioning vessels.

name: Buhr Ailgra
description: Founded by Éothraim, circa T.A. 1380.. Defense against Easterling attacks, a trading center for Eothraim.. Buhr Ailgra is one of the few permanent settlements of the Eothraim, who follow the migrations of their herds through the plains of Rhovanion. The town is a simple settlement of thatch and turf houses surrounded by a stout earthen rampart.

name: Barak-shathur
description: Founded by settlers from Azanulinbar-dûm c. T.A. 1490.. Exploitation of the mineral resources of the Iron Hills.. The Khuzdul name of the stronghold is thought to mean 'Axe Mountain' in the Westron.

name: Lond Daer
description: Established as a colony of Númenor by Aldarion in the ninth century of the Second Age.. A Númenórean haven on the shores of Endor.. Lond Daer was ruined by a cataclysmic tidal wave in S.A. 3319.

name: Tilmendir
description: Built by Gondor, circa T.A. 991.. Surveillance of traffic passing from Westmarch to Anfalas; a show of authority for the local Dunnish tribes.. Tilmendir is located ten miles south of the town Derwath.

name: Dundok
description: Occupied by Hobbits since at least T.A. 1360.. Agriculture.. The village is hidden within the drier reaches of the Gladden Fields.

name: Iant Methed
description: Iant Methed (The Last Bridge) was built of stone with three great arches at the height of Arnorian power in T.A. 320. In the petty wars of Rhudaur the crossing was generally considered neutral ground. The Last Inn, long run by the Grumm family, stood nearby; just northwest lay the ruins of Dol Elerilde, stronghold of an old lord of Rhudaur.

name: Laur Dunnen
description: Larach Duhnnan lay on territory of Clan Feargan but the flow of foreign traffic made it a neutral ground for inter-tribal discussions and trade. It had no walls, relying on the nearby Feargan fortress for defense, and sat at one of the few accessible crossings of the Dunstrem, for which the clan lords charged a modest toll.

name: Greenholm
description: A quiet outpost in the middle Third Age, home to a few Arthadan families coexisting with nearby Elves who observed the comings and goings of mortals on behalf of the lords of the Grey Havens. One of the Elves, Sinda Calenlinn, was an expert herbalist fond of fermented grains.

name: Enerond
description: Ruined city. Enerond was a township of Brethyar in Arthedain, on the east bank of the Lune River. It had a population of about 5,100 in T.A. 250, declining through the Third Age until abandoned before its end.

name: Eithel Thurin
description: Founded by Gondorian army, circa T.A. 932.. Center for Gondorian control in Harondor; trading center for coastal traffic.. The town is frequently the target of raids from Umbar and hostile Haradrim.

name: Towers Of The Teeth
description: Begun by Gondor in T.A. 1.. Observation of traffic attempting to pass into or out of Mordor.. Two towers, Narchost and Carchost, built on either side of the Cirith Gorgor stand as a guard on Mordor.

name: Andrath
description: Founded circa T.A. 860 by Thorondur of Cardolan.. A center of regional administration; the seat of the Prince of Dol Tinare from T.A. 1410 to T.A. 1639.. Andrath has been ruined and rebuilt many times over the course of its history. Most recently, it was sacked by a Hillman party led by King Broggha of Rhudaur in 1639.

name: Bar-en-tinnen
description: Settled circa T.A. 1512.. Provision of inland communities with seafood; a trading center for local farmers.. Situated on the east bank of the Anduin, Bar-en-tinnen grew up around the mill on the Sir Tinnen.

name: Onodrith
description: Grew around ferry in tenth century of the Third Age, granted a royal charter in T.A. 1240.. Small market town at crossing of River Entwash; a local administrative center.. Onodrith is a station for most of the river traffic to Gondor's northern and western provinces.

name: Minas Arthor
description: Minas Arthor, once the capital of Arnach, was situated at the crossings of the Erui, where a settlement had existed since the earliest chronicles. Founded at the beginning of the Third Age, it grew into the center of a prosperous agricultural district.

name: Bar Astirian
description: Astorion Manor (S. Bâr Astorion) was the former home of House Astorion, a noble Gondorian family. It was situated on the outskirts of the Entwash Mouths, north of Min-Rimmon, where the family held extensive lands.

name: Nothva Rhaglaw
description: Founded by Broggha of Rhudaur in T.A. 1639.. A trading center; a military point of muster; local administration.. The only permanent Hillman town west of the Mitheithel, Nothva Rhaglaw is a trading center and a source of local justice.

name: Gram
description: The Witch-King's servants erected this mountain fortress on the southern branch of the Misty Mountains forming the boundary of Nan Angmar. Gram was the most formidable Orc stronghold west of the mountains; no man had ever escaped its depths to give a coherent account of its interior.

name: Bar Engui
description: A small hamlet on the river Engui in Andrast, one of the most remote corners of Gondor. A handful of stone-walled garths cluster near the river's mouth; the people here are Dunir fishermen and small farmers who seldom see strangers. The name means 'Dwelling on the Engui' in Sindarin.

name: Fangoth
description: Ruined city.

name: Minas Tirith
description: Originally called Minas Anor (S. Tower of the Sun), Minas Tirith was built at the foot of Mount Mindolluin in the Ered Nimrais. The city rose in seven concentric tiers of white stone, each gate offset from the last, and the White Tower housed the last Palantír in Gondor's keeping. Together with Osgiliath and Minas Ithil, it formed the core of Gondor.

name: Ossarnen
description: Settled by Gondorians around S.A. 3350; granted a royal charter in T.A. 792.. A supply center for mining communities in the White Mountains.. Ossarnen is predominantly a mining community situated well off the main road through inland Gondor.

name: Dunlostir
description: First market recorded in T.A. 808.. A gathering place for the farmers of central Calenardhon; grain storage.. Dunlostir is the center of the region's cereal trade.

name: Mithlond
description: Founded by Cirdan after the Fall of Beleriand.. The manufacture of ships; a departure point for Elves leaving Middle-earth.. Two harbors, Mithannúnlond on the west shore of the Gulf of Lhûn and Mithrónalond on the east, make up the city.

name: Argonath
description: Finished in T.A. 1340 by Romendacil II.. Designation of the northern boundary of Gondor.. Carved rocks flanking the chasm at the northern entrance to Nen Hithoel. The figures represent Isildur and Anárion. Since the time of Romendacil II, Gondor's northern border has moved even farther north, making the pillars obsolete. Nonetheless, they are a magnificent spectacle.

name: Gundabad
description: Mount Gundabad was a sacred site of the Dwarves and an important enclave in the northern Misty Mountains. It was seized by Orcs multiple times and eventually became the capital of the northern Orc-realm, a constant threat to the free peoples of the north.

name: Nadior
description: Ruined city. Nadior was a settlement of Ethir Gwathló in Cardolan, in a small bay on the Minhiriath coast about seventy miles west of the Gwathló mouths. It had a population of roughly 4,900 in T.A. 250, declining through the mid-Third Age until finally abandoned by T.A. 1640.

name: Tirith Nindir
description: An ancient Gondorian fortress built to protect and guard the approaches to the Dagorlad plain.

name: Lond Angren
description: Lond Angren (S. Iron Port) began as a small Númenórean outpost of the Second Age and grew into a city and lordship controlling western Enedwaith near the mouth of the River Angren.

name: Bar-en-tinnen
description: Settled circa T.A. 1512.. Provision of inland communities with seafood; a trading center for local farmers.. Situated on the east bank of the Anduin, Bar-en-tinnen grew up around the mill on the Sir Tinnen.

name: Fanuilond
description: Continuous settlement from at least T.A. 2.. Primarily a fishing village; also handles shipping on the Ethir Anduin.. Fanuilond has repeatedly been the target of Corsair attacks, but its prime location keeps the population returning.

name: Archet
description: Settled in T.A. 1420 by residents of Bree-land.. Agriculture; an economic focal point for farmers who live a moderate distance from Bree.. Archet lies on the edge of the Chetwood and is devoted exclusively to agriculture.

name: Osgiliath
description: Founded by Elendil in S.A. 3320.. The former capital of Gondor; a center for learning and art; the major port for Anduin traffic.. Osgiliath has been plagued by disaster. Castamir's supporters burned the city during the Kin-strife.

name: Bar Lithyrn
description: Founded by Gondorian commercial interests in T.A. 1590.. Extraction of mineral ores from northern Ered Lithui.. Most of the inhabitants are local Donaen lured to the mountains by the prospect of wealth. Their Dúnadan lords mine silver here for Gondor's treasuries. The town suffers from isolation, and the population shrinks each year as the silver veins dry up.

name: Belegost
description: The First Age, possibly before the Sun and the Moon first rose.. Exploit the riches of the Ered Luin, defense against all enemies.. A few bold Dwarven explorers have recently begun to explore the ruins in hopes of finding the artifacts of their ancestors.

name: Lond Galen
description: The capital of Anfalas and its largest town, set near the mouth of the Celgalen where the river meets the Bay of Belfalas. About 5,000 inhabitants work as merchants, artisans, sailors and officials; most also tend plots outside the town for their daily bread. The town's major building is the governor's small manor; a few noble townhouses stand taller than the usual one- or two-storey stone buildings. A fifteen-foot wall (rebuilt in T.A. 1652 after Corsair raids became endemic) encloses the settled area, and a small fort guards the southern approach.

name: Porosir
description: A Gondorian castle and lordly manor in southwestern Ithilien, on the northern banks of the Poros. It is the western frontier seat of House Faron, whose domain guards this stretch of the borderlands. The castle is a working military manor of dressed stone, its courtyard always busy with horses and men moving on the Poros road.

name: Tumnogoth Iaur
description: The Dwarves of Tumnogath were poor and secretive. Many of their young had emigrated to the distant Iron Hills, and the community stagnated over a few generations. The remaining Dwarven population consisted largely of treasure-seekers and travelling tinkers.

name: Esgaroth
description: Founded as independent town on the site of a seasonal trading station, circa T.A. 1240.. A market for most goods passing through northern Rhovanion; a regional focal point for rural Northmen.. Esgaroth is built upon great platforms above the Annen (Long Lake) and is connected to land by a single bridge.

name: Edoras
description: Edoras (R. The Courts) was the capital of Rohan, built upon an ancient hill site at the northern edge of the White Mountains. The great hall Meduseld crowned the hill, and the kings of the Mark were buried in twin rows of barrows outside the main gate. The surrounding treeless plains gave its lookouts a commanding view of all approaches.

name: Firaen
description: Ruined city.

name: Dale
description: Held by Lords of Dale since T.A. 1109.. The seat of the Thyn; a center for manufacturing products for the rural Northmen.. This town of Urban Northmen is set in a sheltered valley beneath Erebor.

name: Litash
description: Founded by the Witch-king soon after T.A. 1300.. A training center for priests of the Dark Religion; a base for tax collection in eastern Angmar.. Litash guards the eastern approaches to Angmar, and the Witch-king stations a large garrison here.

name: Bottoms Bridge
description: Settled circa T.A. 1337.. A supply center for traders sailing on the Anduin.. Bottoms Bridge is located on a small island at the edge of the Gladden Marshes.

name: Forlond
description: Founded after the Ruin of Beleriand.. Shipbuilding; a refuge for the Eldar.. The Elvish haven in Forlindon is hidden and protected by a steep fjord.

name: Aglarond
description: First used as a refuge by Númenóreans c. S.A. 3331.. Refuge and storehouse for the garrison at Ostiras.. A system of extensive caverns in the northern slopes of the White Mountains. An untapped wealth of gems here serves as an emergency financial reserve for Gondor's Kings.

name: Faemar
description: Faemar was a village set midway along the long, narrow Taur Galen, at a ford crossing the river. The steep valley downstream made farming impossible; villagers subsisted on wild game, berries, herbs, nuts, and wood for charcoal.

name: Ambon Noirinyaron
description: Ruined mansion.

name: Woodmen Village
description: A small village of the Woodmen in the Upper Anduin Vales, partially built on large platforms in the trees in the Elven fashion, reflecting the friendly relations between the Woodmen and the nearby Wood-Elves.

name: Arailt
description: Arailt Hold was the ancient seat of the Dunlending Arailt clan. By the time of the War of the Ring it had long been abandoned; the Rohirrim had established a scouting camp above the ruins and nearby caves.

name: Fennas Drunnin
description: A Council directed most affairs in Fennas Drúnin, a town that depended on local farms and vineyards for its prosperity. Some Hobbits lived nearby but generally avoided contact with the townspeople. Far from the main roads of Cardolan, it was a good base for anyone wishing to explore southern Rhudaur or the Elven ruins of Eregion.

name: Faranel
description: Ruined city.

name: Rildolach
description: Ruined city.

name: Malehaut
description: A coastal Eriadorian town of Fonntir in Minhiriath, known as Atem ('fathom') or Carmalehaute ('Malehaut's town') in Dunnish. By T.A. 2740 it had a population of some 2,700 — a working port with fishermen, traders and those who live by the sea's rhythms.

name: Aerost
description: A coastal fortress on the Rathon Gondor in Anfalas, some forty miles west of Lond Galen. Built after the Great Raid on Pelargir (T.A. 1634) to defend the coast between the Celgalen and the Lefnui, Aerost is a key link in Anfalas's chain of coastal beacon towers. From its walls, watchmen can warn neighbouring settlements of Corsair raids within hours. The fort's stone battlements are weathered by salt wind, and a small settlement of soldiers and their families huddles in its shadow.

name: Herubar Gûlar
description: Unknown, possibly Númenórean in origin.. Control of traffic on and along the Mitheithel.. The keep is reputed to be haunted.

name: Anghul
description: Anghul ('Iron-wind' in Sindarin) is one of the six great fortresses of Angmar, situated in the Witch-kingdom's north on a hill above the Anghir, below the escarpment of Nan Angmar. It broods over the Anghir Vale and controls the No Man's Land beyond. A garrison of 300 men keeps watch for spies attempting to penetrate Angmar's central plateau; the commander is one of the Witch-king's Ashâktur. Dark stone walls and watchtowers; the wind off the escarpment is bitter and constant. The Black Raven on Red is the banner of the garrison here.

name: Standelf
description: A small hamlet in southern Buckland, close to the High Hay and the Old Forest. Originally built to house miners working the local delving ('Stangedelf'), it grew into a small Hobbit village of stockbreeders. Most inhabitants are a poorer branch of the Brandybuck clan; the Goldworthies, Gorbucks, Headstrongs and Puddyfoots are other families here. The Old Forest looms to the south.

name: Haysend
description: A small Hobbit village at the southern end of Buckland, where the High Hay meets the Brandywine's marshy mouth. The Withywindle swamp begins just beyond; Bucklanders from Haysend, Breredon and Grindwall fish these swamps and can be hired to take travellers downriver. Buckland's shirriffs gather here twice a week to patrol and maintain the High Hay. The settlement is small but watchful, its people accustomed to the odd noises that come from the Old Forest side of the river.

name: Tookbank
description: A Hobbit market town in Tookland, associated with the Took family. Its market brings farmers and craftsmen from across the surrounding area on market days.

name: Serelond
description: A town of Anfalas on the Celgalen, with about 1,300 inhabitants in small industry, local trade and subsistence farming. Stone houses of one or two storeys line the streets; weekly market; annual fair. The town hall is the tallest building. A typical, orderly Gondorian provincial settlement.

name: Shedûn
description: Built by Witch-king circa 1310.. Regional administration; the control of traffic into and out of Nan Angmar.. The broad plains below Shedûn provide a training ground and a mustering point for the Witch-king's Orcish forces.

name: Angrenost
description: First used as a stronghold by Númenóreans around S.A. 1750, completed by Gondor in T.A. 14.. Control of traffic passing through Gap of Calenardhon; administration of northwest Gondor.. The impenetrable tower of Orthanc forms the core of the defenses, with a vast circular wall one mile in diameter providing the first line of security. The earth itself formed the citadel, for the outer ring is a volcanic cone shaped by the Men of Númenor, and the spire of Orthanc is in fact a pillar of glossy black volcanic rock known as laen. Angrenost now represents the northwestern extent of effective Gondorian power. The King of Gondor maintains a large garrison here to keep the warlike tribes of Dunland in check. Travellers on the Old North Road frequently make a side trip to Angrenost to exploit its fine and fully stocked reprovisioning facilities.

name: Dol Gwaer
description: A lordly manor of the Nan Celgalen in western Anfalas, seat of a minor Gondorian noble family. Its stone hall and outbuildings sit in a wooded valley sheltered from the sea-wind; the surrounding estate grows grain and raises sheep. A small household of retainers and tenants keeps the manor in order.

name: Caras Galadhon
description: Designed by Galadriel in S.A. 1601.. A safe haven for all Elven folk.. A fortified yet beautiful city in the heart of Lórien. It is a network of interconnected flets supported by the massive trunks of great mallorn trees.

name: Lond Thalos
description: Lond Thalos (S. Port of Thalos) served as both a medical and herbal community and as a timber port supplying wood to the shipyards of the Grey Havens.

name: Hwalord
description: A town in the region, its stone houses and wharves serving the commerce of the surrounding area. Details of its exact character depend on the local economy and lord.

name: Tharagdonrost
description: A city in the region whose size and character make it a significant centre of population and trade.

name: Talsir
description: Talsir was one of the free cities of Cardolan on the west bank of the Gwathló, about 170 miles downstream from Tharbad. With a population of roughly 4,300 in T.A. 250, it was an important river stop and one of the few settlements of ancient Minhiriath to survive into the late Third Age.

name: Scary
description: Settled during the migration into the Shire, T.A. 1601-1610.. Agriculture; mining; quarries.. Set in the rugged country of the Eastfarthing, Scary and its nearby mines provide most of the tin and copper ores used in the Shire.

name: Overhill
description: A new Hobbit village north of Screaming Hill, settled by the Fallohide Barleycorn clan and the Harfoot Beechkins, both of whom came to the Shire after being persecuted by brigands. The two clans share a suspicion of Big Folk that occasionally turns hostile; other Hobbits are uneasy about the disappearances of Men who venture into the angle between Baining Stream and Screaming Hill. In later, more peaceful times, Overhill becomes a comfortable farming community; the Bagginses own much of the surrounding land.

name: Framsburg
description: Wraecaburg, though not directly under the Witch-King's rule, suffered frequent attacks and harbored many unsavory characters from outside Rhovanion. Its men occasionally traded with kinsmen at Maethelburg, but relations grew increasingly suspicious over time.

name: Endrist
description: Ruined city.

name: Annulond
description: The principal town of Pinnath Gelin, set at the tip of a tongue of land where a stream from the Ram Galen joins the Lefnui. The folk of Annúlond maintain the only bridge spanning the Lefnui in this region. The town is modest but orderly: small stone houses of one or two storeys line the streets, and the lieutenant-Cáno's large house stands beside the open main square that slopes down to the river wharf. Since the area lies in Gondor's peaceful interior, no town wall was ever built. Outside the walls, the Knight of Annúlond's manor overlooks liegemen's farms and tenant plots.

name: Woodhall
description: A Hobbit village in the Eastfarthing on the northern eaves of the Woody End, founded in T.A. 1612 by Harfoots of the Staghorn and Huckle clans on the site of an old Eriadorian village. The earlier settlement was destroyed in T.A. 1409 when Orc wolf-riders slaughtered its inhabitants. The new Hobbit village sits where those same apple orchards still bear fruit and mushrooms grow on shaded terraces. A glade of the Elven Wandering Companies lies nearby; Elves are sometimes seen passing through at dusk.

name: Carn Dûm
description: First delved by Dwarves in the early T.A, occupied by the Witch-king ca. T.A. 1300.. Last line of defense for the Witch-king; a base from which he plots the downfall of Arthedain.. Situated at the northwestern tip of the Misty Mountains, only a handful of the Free Peoples have viewed the interior of Carn Dûm and escaped alive.

name: Eruimar
description: Eruimar (S. Lonely Home) sat at the foot of the Ramluin (S. Blue Wall), below the easternmost point where the Ered Luin foothills gave way to rocky cliffs. Despite its remote setting, distance rather than forest or enchantment created its isolation. The village held no more than thirty-five households; founded by the Dúnedain, most of its roughly 150 residents were River-men or Northmen by the mid-Third Age.

name: Haerlond
description: Originally settled by Númenórean colonists in the Second Age, granted a royal charter by Turambar of Gondor in T.A. 602.. Reprovisioning facility for ships rounding Cape of Andrast; fishing village.. Haerlond's small harbor services ships damaged in the dangerous passage around the cape.

name: Annon Wain
description: Annon Wain, in the upper Lefnui valley, was a local market center. To the north lay extensive mines whose ore was shipped downriver to Annúlond, while nearby firs from the Ered Nimrais supplied Gondor's naval yards. The town itself was modest — merchant houses, simple dwellings, and riverside warehouses — with a population of roughly 2,500.

name: Thoronbar
description: Ruined village.

name: Cerin Balgrist
description: Ruined city.

name: Andrath
description: Founded circa T.A. 860 by Thorondur of Cardolan.. A center of regional administration; the seat of the Prince of Dol Tinare from T.A. 1410 to T.A. 1639.. Andrath has been ruined and rebuilt many times over the course of its history. Most recently, it was sacked by a Hillman party led by King Broggha of Rhudaur in 1639.

name: Lorilad
description: Lórilad was a fortified village of Lebennin, situated in the Ethir Anduin roughly seventy-five miles downstream from Pelargir, halfway between Pelargir and the western cape of Lebennin.

name: Sindbar
description: A small Gondorian town near the westernmost eaves of Drúadan Forest, about eight miles north of Mount Nardol. Its stone houses and quiet market serve the scattered farms and woodcutters of the area. The Drúadan Forest nearby is avoided by most travellers; the Woses are seldom seen but always suspected to be watching.

name: Larad
description: Ruined city.

name: Buzdum Mazar
description: The greatest mine of Nan-i-Naugrim and the capital of the Broadbeam Dwarves since the Second Age. By the mid-Third Age, Edennogrod — the largely ruined Khazad above Nan-i-Naugrim — dominated the area. At the end of the Third Age it was governed by the feudal lord Grár, who bore the honorary title Mountain King among the Shire-folk.

name: Minas Brethil
description: Minas Brethil (S. Silver Birch Tower), capital of Celossien, was situated in the upper Serni valley and served as an agricultural center between the Glinduin and Serni rivers. After the Kin-strife it held some 4,000 inhabitants, a number halved by the Great Plague and only slowly recovered thereafter.

name: Durthang
description: Built by Isildur, T.A. 1.. Surveillance of traffic crossing the plain of Udûn.. The fortress rests on a precarious ledge high above Udûn, giving it an excellent view of any trespassers into Mordor.

name: Longbottom
description: A Hobbit village in the Southfarthing, historically significant as the first place in the Shire where pipe-weed was cultivated. Around S.R. 1070 (T.A. 2670), Tobold Hornblower of Longbottom brought the pipe-weed plant from Bree and began growing it in his gardens, giving rise to the famous 'Longbottom Leaf' strain. The village lies in a gentle valley of the Southfarthing and its fields are given over largely to pipe-weed cultivation, the primary industry of the region.

name: Long Cleeve
description: The Shire community with the best access to the Hills of Evendim and the Utter North. Long Cleeve is a lively market town that trades in furs, ivory, antler and deer-hide brought in by Rangers, hill-folk and trappers from beyond the bounds. The Northwatch shirriffs — who carry bows and spears rather than the usual staves — are headquartered here. A coppice nearby shelters a monument to Bandobras Took, hero of the Battle of Greenfields, who is also the town's most famous former resident.

name: Melburg
description: Maethelburg (Meeting Fort) controlled virtually all trade through the upper Anduin valley and produced large quantities of tin from the lower Hithaeglir mines. Travelers heading west stocked up before crossing the Misty Mountains; those arriving from the east rested here. Built on an artificial mound behind a massive timber palisade, it harbored an unusually high number of spies due to its proximity to Angmar and the Orc-holds of Gundabad.

name: Iach Sarn
description: Iach Sarn clustered about the base of a limestone hill, surrounded by small farms and hardwood groves. On the south bank, just downstream of the main road, lay the ruins of an older village of earth-and-stone construction. Weathered steps led down a rubble embankment to the riverside docks, lined with unpainted ferries and fishing boats.

name: Cithrenor
description: Ruined town.

name: Maegoth
description: Ruined city.

name: Enerond
description: Ruined city.

name: Fanduin
description: Fanduin had a fairly large population of 4,100 in T.A. 250, but its numbers declined through the middle Third Age and the settlement was eventually abandoned before the end of the Third Age.

name: Pelargir
description: Pelargir (S. Garth of Royal Ships) was founded by the Faithful of Númenor in S.A. 2350 and became the chief harbour and oldest city of Gondor, built on the Anduin at the mouth of the Sirith. It served as the base of Gondor's great fleet and was the kingdom's primary port of trade, second city only to Minas Tirith.
