name: Cor Angaladh
description: A ritual burial ground, now long disused. Once one of the most sacred sites of the Northmen, this place is now shadowed by the darkness of Mirkwood. Men of the north avoid it, believing it to be cursed.

name: Carrock
description: Carrock was a great rocky islet in the Anduin, between Iach Iaur and Maethelburg, held in deep spiritual reverence by the Beornings who dwelt nearby. The Anduin was shallow here, allowing a ford near the Carrock, though the swift current made the crossing genuinely dangerous.

name: Tuckborough
description: Tuckborough was the ancestral seat of the Took family, located in the Green Hill Country of Westfarthing. The surrounding area included the market center of Whitwell, the woodcraft village of Tuckburrow, and Dolwich, which supplied timber and granite to the southern Farthings.

name: Dol Caranthan
description: A town of Anórien on the west bank of the Anduin, north of Cair Andros, seat of the local noble family of House Caranthôn. Its stone houses face the great river, and the ferry landing here sees regular traffic of soldiers and merchants moving between Anórien and the east bank. The Caranthôn family's hall is the largest building in the settlement.

name: Barad Eldanar
description: An ancient Arnorian castle built on the eastern edge of the Dyr Úvadan, ancestral home of House Eldanar — one of Elendil's Faithful who settled here in the early Third Age. The castle is one of the oldest Númenórean fortifications in northwestern Middle-earth, rebuilt to its current imposing form in the early years of Arnor. By c. T.A. 1325, the Angmarim seized it; the rightful lord Elenuil escaped through a secret tunnel and the family has dwelt in Fornost since, regarding the castle's occupation as a deep shame. A well-armed and dangerous Angmarim garrison now holds the walls.

name: Lond-in-Telerrim
description: A Falathrin Elvish haven on the western coast, home to a community of Telerin Elves who raise sheep and work the sea. In the nearby Taur-in-Telerrim ('Wood of the Teleri') persistent rumours circulate of a small drake lurking in the deep forest — though no search has ever found its lair. The haven's whitewashed stone buildings face the water; the smell of lanolin and salt is everywhere.

name: Buhr Mahrling
description: Conquered by Northmen, T.A. 1503.. A trading base for Northmen on the lower River Celduin.. Most of the residents here are Éothraim who use it as a seasonal base, but there is also a sizeable population of Northmen from Esgaroth who seek an advance trading outpost near Dorwinion.

name: Endil
description: Built by Anárion, circa S.A. 3398.. A watch-post for pirates and raiders; a warning beacon to ships passing close to the rocky shoals.. The Prince of Dor-en-Ernil maintains a series of towers along the Belfalas coast.

name: Carach Angren
description: Raised by Sauron in the Second Age, rebuilt by Gondor in T.A. 42.. Control of traffic travelling through the pass connecting Udûn and Gorgoroth.. The original wall at Carach Angren was built by Sauron and destroyed by the Last Alliance.

name: Methrast
description: Methrast is the southernmost cape of the Belfalas peninsula — sheer, unclimbable cliffs and dangerous shoals that only the most skilled pilots dare approach by sea. The cape also marks one terminus of the Belfalas coastal beacon system; news of approaching enemies sighted here can reach Dol Amroth in hours. Seals sun themselves on the rocky shores below. There is no settlement at the cape itself, only a small watch-post.

name: Bar-en-Dinnen
description: Bar-en-Dinnen (S. Dwelling on the Dinnen) was a fishing village and river-trading town in southern Ithilien, about sixty miles south of Minas Tirith, at the junction of the Tinnen and Anduin rivers. As the only significant settlement in Harithilien north of Hyarpende, it was an important hub for river commerce between Ithilien and Lebennin.

name: Mirolos
description: Mirolos (S. Jewel-Dream; also Londalos) was a Noldor settlement founded by followers of the Sons of Fëanor in the first decade of the Second Age, the largest community in the Hûb Conath region. Founded by Maglor before his disappearance, it was burned during the Long Winter when a northern dragon entered the area.

name: Forondir
description: Ruined city.

name: Anhamar
description: Anhamar is the local name for the region around the fortress of Anghul in northern Angmar — a bleak highland above the Anghir Vale where the Witch-king's watchers keep their eyes on the No Man's Land. Windswept moorland and dark rock; no settlement of note, only military outposts and the occasional patrol of the Witch-king's men.

name: Hobbiton
description: Hobbiton was a prominent Hobbit village in the Westfarthing of the Shire, north of the Great East Road. It was home to Bag End on the Hill — the residence of Bilbo Baggins and later Frodo Baggins.

name: Pinnornost
description: A border fortress of Anfalas guarding the path of the Len Lefnui at the mouth of the Nan-i-Feryth, named for its position on a pine-clad ridge. Pinnornost was built in T.A. 823 to hold the pass on the frontier with the Daen clans of Haerawreigion. A beacon tower communicates with a sister tower thirty miles east. By T.A. 2157 the garrison was judged too costly to maintain; the fortress was locked up and has slowly fallen into disrepair since.

name: Vinyathrad
description: Edenathrad ('New Ford') is a ford and village north of the ancient Sarn Athrad on the Gelion. It replaced the older ford for practical traffic between Forlindon and the eastern lands. The village is a small settlement of stone and timber houses near the riverbank; Amboron is its chieftain. Traders and travellers cross here regularly.

name: Caras Tolfalas
description: Claimed by Gondor since S.A. 3321; first settled by royal command in T.A. 934.. Subsidized by Gondor's King more to maintain a claim on the Isle of Tolfalas than for economic reasons.. A poor harbor limits access to this town, but it is the only sure mooring on Tolfalas.

name: Buhr Waldmarh
description: Founded by Northmen, circa T.A. 1290.. A stable base for the Withras clan; an economic focus for the surrounding farms.. This typical Northman town has many timber longhouses and an earthen dike surmounted by a wooden palisade.

name: Buhr Widu
description: Founded by Vidugavia in T.A. 1249.. A center for administration for the Waildung clan; a collecting point for the surrounding Gramuz farms.. Buhr Widu, by the side of the small lake known as Woodmere, retains some of the royal splendor from the time of Vidugavia.

name: Barad Garan
description: Ruined city

name: Cum Arelaid
description: Ruins. An ancient burial complex of pre-Númenórean tumuli and stone-lined graves, sacred to the early peoples of the region.

name: Ethraid Engrin
description: The Fords of Isen were the main crossing point for those traversing the Gap of Rohan, with no bridge spanning the powerful river. The Rohirrim patrolled the area as their primary western gateway; Dunlending hillmen often seized the fords and demanded tolls. Three rows of broad flat stones crossed the waters, with graded horse-fords between them.

name: Annodrun
description: Ruined city.

name: Rhosgobel
description: Built by Radagast around T.A. 1100.. A place of healing and herb-lore; a refuge from Mirkwood; an observation post on Dol Guldur.. Rhosgobel is a simple place formed out of the boughs of a great furry oak tree.

name: Argonath
description: Finished in T.A. 1340 by Romendacil II.. Designation of the northern boundary of Gondor.. Carved rocks flanking the chasm at the northern entrance to Nen Hithoel. The figures represent Isildur and Anárion. Since the time of Romendacil II, Gondor's northern border has moved even farther north, making the pillars obsolete. Nonetheless, they are a magnificent spectacle.

name: Amon Sûl
description: Originally built by Elendil in the Second Age.. Repository for a Palantír; later served as a landmark at the juncture of the three Kingdoms of the North.. From this vantage point, one has an excellent view of the Great East Road as it heads towards Rhudaur. For this reason, the hill served as a strategic focus for western Arthedain and Cardolan and has a long history of conflict surrounding it. The tower of Amon Sûl once held Palantír, which was removed to Fornost following the invasion by Angmar in T.A. 1409.

name: Ahus
description: A rough village of the Fell-men in Angmar, near the fortress of Angsûl. Its squat stone-and-timber huts sit in the shadow of the Witch-kingdom's northern hills, home to a tribe loyal to the Witch-king. The settlement smells of smoke and animal fat; life here is harsh and martial, and strangers are rarely welcome.

name: Phaluran
description: A township of the Tulban district of Angmar, built in the mid-Third Age and home to about 3,500 at its peak. The settlement is a rough frontier place — dark timber houses in the shadow of the Angmar hills, its people bound by the Witch-king's will. With the fall of Angmar, the town is eventually abandoned. By the late Third Age only ruins remain: dark and windswept, avoided by all.

name: Dinach
description: A hilltop town in Mintyrnath, noted as two days' walk northwest of Tharbad. Its Sindarin name means 'Silent Spine'; it sits on a strategic spur overlooking the junction of the Sendiel and Andeithel rivers, where an old Arnorian stone bridge — the Iant Dinach — still spans the water. In the mid-Third Age, Dinach is a rough and somewhat dangerous waystation: a small Tinereb garrison holds the old tower on Dol Dinach, and the fifty-odd stone buildings along the Men Formen shelter travellers, smugglers and agents of various factions. The low-lying fields along the river flood every spring.

name: Dol Occum
description: A hill-tower on the Men Formen in southern Arthedain, within the village of Occum. The tower is a Dúnedain watch-post commanding the road; the surrounding village of timber and stone houses provides the garrison with supplies and labour. The name derives from an older Westron name meaning 'ox-valley', and cattle are still the main wealth of the area.

name: Morva Tarth
description: The town of Móin Dúldachd ('Marsh of Mist') shares its name with the surrounding peat bogs of northern Rhudaur. A single track threads the swamp; those who leave it risk hidden bogs that are virtually bottomless. The town itself is the centre of what economic life exists in the Ettendales, and its chief fortification — the Kaldabaurg — was once the citadel of Northman mercenaries who served the Dúnedain. A grim, isolated place.

name: Cargash
description: Built by Witch-king, ca. T.A. 1400.. Designed to give the Witch-king warning of trespassers and a base from which to strike at Arthedain.. Regular patrols depart from Mindil Cargash to sweep En Udanoriath which lies between Angmar and Arthedain.

name: Galibur
description: Settled during Númenórean expansion in Gondor, circa S.A. 2500.. An important Dunnish ritual site in central Belfalas peninsula.. Ruins dating to the time of the Daen Coentis lie just north of town.

name: Grimsly Dale
description: A Harfoot farming village located in a bend of the Fairwater River,quiet place, famous for its flowers . Although few remembered it , there had once been a scenic trail in the area that was a popular sidetrip on the Woodland Tour. Reason for its popularity could be discerned in the dense concentration of berry bushes and flowering plants along the riverside paths . The village got its name , however, from the bones of men and horses that also turned up along the trail . A large party of Dúnedain were ambushed here during the Scourging of T.A . 1409

name: Bankling
description: Known locally as 'Bankling' and later Whitfurrows, this good-sized Hobbit town sits squarely on the Great East Road in the midst of the best farmland of the Eastfarthing. Heavy wagons cross the Scary Pike at Budge Ford to the north, while a fine wooden bridge takes lighter traffic to Waterbury across the river. The town is known for its corn and flax fields and for the Blushing Ivy Tavern, meeting place of the Brow, Pen and Scroll Society — a bookish Hobbit club with a taste for Elvish lore that gives them a reputation for peculiarity among their neighbours.

name: Rushy
description: A riverside village in the Marish, where Rivermen and Stoor Hobbits live uneasily side by side. Euric Thuigildsun, the ancient white-haired patriarch of the local Riverman clans, eyes the new Stoor immigrants with cautious acceptance, hoping for commercial advantage. His rival Thuidimer of nearby Flynettle outmanoeuvres him regularly. Rumours of strange reptilian creatures seen on the marshland trails between Rushy and Flynettle add an uneasy note to an already tense village.

name: Flynettle
description: Flynettle in the mid-Third Age is a rough river-landing and fishing camp on the banks of the Anduin — nothing more than a landing stage and a handful of sheds used by riverboat crews. The old Deep Hallow House is a crumbling ruin on the bank, avoided by locals. The Stoor village of Deephallow lies nearby; Flynettle itself has no permanent inhabitants of note.

name: Willowbottom
description: A village in the Eastfarthing of the Shire, near the Baranduin and the Overbourne Marshes. A secret ford used by Elven Wandering Companies lies near here. The village sits on the Elfwood stream close to where the Thistle Brook flows into the Shirebourn — a marshy, willow-edged landscape of low Hobbit cottages and kitchen gardens.

name: Pincup
description: A quiet Hobbit woodland village in the Southfarthing. The founding Pendle family are long gone; the Goodbodys are now the most prominent residents, though the Tooks are the main landowners. The village has a reputation for being an unusually secluded and woodsy sort of place, and Sîrrandrai Elvish artefacts occasionally turn up in local mathom collections.

name: Wood Junction
description: A Shire village in the Westfarthing, 15 miles west of Bywater on the Great East Road, where the Sarn Ford road meets the main road. Originally called Wood Junction in Arnorian times, later shortened to Waymoot by Hobbits. A natural stopping point: a comfortable inn and a few houses cluster at the crossroads, busy with traffic between Bywater and Bree.

name: Bywater
description: Settled as a crossroads village by Men of Arthedain in the early Third Age; later resettled during Hobbit migrations to the Shire, T.A. 1601-1610. An agricultural market village at the crossroads of the Great East Road, frequented by traders and travellers passing between Hobbiton and the East Farthing. Bywater lies just north of the Great East Road on the banks of a small pool, and is known for the Green Dragon Inn. Inhabitants (c. T.A. 1640): 50% Harfoot, 40% Fallohide, 10% Stoor; population approx. 360.

name: Waterbury
description: A Hobbit village on the north bank of the Fairwater opposite Bankling, providing access to the Great East Road for miners of the Hills of Scary. It has outgrown its twin settlement as Hobbit migration increased. Later it evolves into the village of Budgeford.

name: Brocken Borings
description: A Hobbit village on the western side of the Hills of Scary, centred on copper and tin mines, some dating to Dwarven miners of the Second Age. Hobbits here — chiefly the Bolger clan — use the old mine shafts as smials, ignoring old Riverman stories that the borings are haunted. There have been inexplicable noises, felt presences and glimpsed faces in the dark passages; in a small shed in the park, 'Neblin-nuggets' — copper nodules roughly shaped like sleeping Dwarves — are displayed as curiosities. A remote and self-contained Hobbit community.

name: Dwaling Bank
description: Originally 'Dwola's Bank', a streamside village with access to the Brandywine by a stream through a swamp — a relatively unguarded entry from Arthedain. In earlier times Rivermen here baked charcoal and kept quiet, aware of an Elvish community a few miles to the northwest. After Arthedain's fall, a quiet resolution left the west bank of the Brandywine to the Shire-folk, and the village became a peaceful Hobbit settlement called Dwaling — a stopover for mild-mannered northlanders and a base for river traffic to Rood.

name: Greenfields
description: A market centre for the northern Shire, Greenfields also serves as a meeting place for traders from the Twilight Hills and beyond the bounds. When available, trout from Lake Evendim and pike from the upper Brandywine are sold here; fine woolens woven from North Moors sheep are a speciality, made in sizes for Big Folk as well as Hobbits. The town's Chief Shirriff is tougher than most, dealing with Rangers, Rivermen and hill-trappers who drift in from the north. A coppice east of town shelters a monument to Bandobras Took's victory over the Goblins of Mount Gram.

name: Tying Field
description: A Hobbit village in the Westfarthing, originally called Tying Field — home of the Gamwich, Gammidge and Roper families. The Gamwidge rope-walk is here, and the town's trade in hemp rope is well known. A ford over the Flexen Stream gives access, though the surrounding Mistvale woods have an unsettling reputation. No inn at the ford.

name: Rendwalk and The Briary
description: Waymoot (originally Wood Junction) is a Shire village in the Westfarthing on the Great East Road, 15 miles west of Bywater, where the main road from Sarn Ford joins the Great East Road. A natural stopping point for travellers; a modest inn and some houses cluster at the crossroads. The name changed from the Arnorian 'Wood Junction' to the Hobbit 'Waymoot' over the centuries.

name: Rond Morvin
description: A secluded Arthadan manor in a river-valley west of the North Moors, close to the North Farthing of the Shire. Rond Morvin controls the ford where the Men Lindon crosses the Barluin Stream; it serves as a gathering point for local fairs and militia musters. The manor is a solid stone house with stables and barns, its household accustomed to hosting travellers and muster-parties.

name: Telwaen
description: Once a large township of 5,000 in the Girithlin highlands of Minhiriath, called Thuilwaen ('Stained helmets'). By T.A. 1400 its population had collapsed and the town was abandoned. What remains today is a ruin amid the highland scrub, its stone walls tumbled and its lanes grown over. Locals do not linger.

name: Eldanar
description: Built by Númenórean colonists around S.A. 2800, seized by Angmar in T.A. 1325.. An advance watch-station for Angmar.. Built originally by the Dúnedain of Rhudaur, Eldanar is now a secure watch-station set well out into the plain known as the En Udanoriath.

name: Bottoms Bridge
description: Settled circa T.A. 1337.. A supply center for traders sailing on the Anduin.. Bottoms Bridge is located on a small island at the edge of the Gladden Marshes.

name: Derndingle
description: Used by Ents since the First Age.. A place of gathering and debate.. Derndingle is a dish-shaped valley with a small, tree-topped mound in the center.

name: Lildamar
description: A town in the region whose character is shaped by local geography and trade routes.

name: Bauruin
description: Gobel Paurruin ('Village of the Firefist') was once a thriving township on the Uialduin, about seventy miles northwest of Annúminas. At its height in the early Third Age it held some 3,600 inhabitants. By T.A. 1400 it had been abandoned after a long population decline. What a traveller finds today is a ghost of moss-covered foundations and broken walls reclaimed by scrub and willow — locals call it a haunted place and do not linger after dark.

name: Chalnen
description: A village that served as a base for Saerlann's northernmost outpost along the Minsiril. In troubled times it is a rough place, known to mercenaries as 'Vice-town': a refuge for camp-followers, spies, deserters and opportunists who cluster here when armies are in the field. The local Saerlannish chieftain Cyllin is a cautious man who does business with whoever pays. Stone huts and muddy lanes; a few alehouses that never close.

name: Ilfin
description: Once a township of 3,100 on the coast of Cardolan, Dol Ilfin was abandoned by T.A. 1640 following the Great Plague and regional collapse. A traveller arriving here in the mid-Third Age finds only weather-beaten ruins on the coastal slope — empty windows in crumbling walls, and gulls nesting where people once lived.

name: Cor Wilishar
description: A Nandorin settlement built on a high crag above the Baranduin and the Nenglos, its name meaning 'Round-hill of the Airy-stone'. The village was abandoned in the years following the Great Plague and the collapse of Cardolan (c. T.A. 1700). By the mid-Third Age, travellers approaching the crag find only the stone footings of old platforms and the rusted remains of rope-ladders; the Nandor who once lived here are gone, and no one has replaced them.

name: Helegnel
description: Once a free town of Cardolan, Gobel Helegnel was a settlement of some 4,100 people in the early Third Age, lying in the central lands of Dol Tinereb about 110 miles west of Tharbad. By T.A. 1400 its population had dwindled and the town was abandoned. What a traveller finds in the mid-Third Age is a landscape of crumbling stone walls and empty hearths, slowly disappearing under woodland regrowth.

name: Caras Denethora
description: An Elvish flet-village of central Harlindon, founded before the rising of the Sun by Danitharo — last king of the Green Elves. Hidden in the trackless ancient woods, it survived the Orc hunts of the First Age unscathed. In the Third Age it looks much as it did at the end of the First Age: wooden platforms and walkways built high among old trees, connected by rope-bridges, the whole community visible only from above or when the lanterns are lit at dusk. Sindar refugees still shelter here occasionally.

name: Bar-i-Dinuviel
description: A fishing village on the coast of Dor Tinuviel, its name meaning 'Home of the Nightingale' in Sindarin. Stone houses with fishing nets hung to dry face a sheltered cove; the people here fish the coastal waters and trade their catch at the nearest market town. The village is small and tranquil, known mainly to sailors navigating this stretch of coast.

name: Mount Gundabad
description: Orcs have occupied the Mountain since the Ruin of Beleriand.. The capital of the Orcish realm of Gundabad; a military base for operations in Rhovanion.. Mount Gundabad faces the peak Ügûlai across the Den Lóke, the gap between the Hithaeglir and the Ered Mithrin.

name: Morthondost
description: Built by Dúnedain in the late Second Age, granted autonomy in T.A. 569.. Protection and administration of the Morthond Vale; exploitation of mineral resources.. Despite its proximity to the notorious Paths of the Dead, the Morthond Vale remains an important mining center for Gondor.

name: Maethelburg
description: Built by Northman settlers in roughly T.A. 1020.. A focal point for Northman political, social, and economic activity in the Nan Anduin.. Due to its central position, Maethelburg controls virtually all trade in the upper Anduin valley.

name: Haudh-in-gwanur
description: Originally created in the Second Age to house the noble dead among the Men of Twilight.. A ritual burial ground, no longer in use.. Extensive ruins mark the burial place of Bel'zordin, a prince who was slain in times before the Númenóreans came east to found colonies.

name: Minas Anor
description: Founded by Anárion in S.A. 3320, rebuilt by Ostoher in T.A. 420.. Capital and royal residence of Gondor; location of a Palantír; the burial place of Gondor's Kings.. Minas Anor is situated on a great spur of rock at the eastern tip of the White Mountains.

name: Lond Ernil
description: Site of an Elvish watch-tower, built c. S.A. 3440; city planned and constructed by Dúnedain, c. T.A. 830.. Seat for the Prince of Dor-en-Ernil; harbors and building yards for Gondor's navy; a mercantile center.. The city guards one of Gondor's safest harbors, holding the realm's largest fleet outside of Pelargir.

name: Londaroth
description: Settled by Northmen around T.A. 900.. Portage river traffic around the falls at Lindal.. Only a small trading station, two mills, and an inn remain to serve the merchants who must portage their wares over the Falls of Celduin.

name: Rul
description: A town in the region, its stone houses and market serving local farming communities.

name: Rond Rhandir
description: A village on the northern side of Ram Galen, with about fifty families and 300 inhabitants. Most are farmers — liegemen, tenants or small freeholders growing grain and hay. All houses are single-storey stone buildings of one or two rooms, except for the slightly larger two-storey house of the local noble. In winter, the men go to the nearby woods to cut timber, floating logs downstream to mills in the Morthond valley. A market fair is held outside the village after the sheep-shearing.

name: Corgonin
description: A small town beside a tributary of the Morthond in the eastern Pinnath Gelin, Corgonin takes its name — 'Circle of Stones' — from a mysterious monument on a nearby knoll: a tall black obelisk of unmarked stone surrounded by a ring of twelve grey menhirs. No one knows its origin, and the locals largely avoid it, though straying sheep occasionally wander inside the circle. The town itself is a major winter gathering point for the Mesta sheep-droves; the wool trade dominates spring life, with shearers filling every house and a great fair held outside the walls when the work is done. Most houses are small, one- or two-storey stone buildings on unpaved streets.

name: Tirith Cobas
description: An Elvish tower on the western shore of Lond Cobas, founded by Celeborn the Wise as an outpost of Edhellond. It rises atop a tall hill and serves as a lighthouse for passing ships, communicating with Tirith Aear on Dol Amroth across the water by light signals. The tower is used as a meeting place and retreat by the leaders of Edhellond's kindreds. A traveller approaching by sea sees the light long before the tower itself becomes visible.

name: Nineryn
description: The Nineryn ('Wetwoods') are two adjoining, marshy woodlands gathering about the upper Morthond. Foot travel through them is difficult due to the boggy ground; the woods are generally avoided by travellers and claimed as a private hunting preserve by the Lord of Mornan. Poaching is heavily fined. No settlement exists here — the Nineryn are a landmark and a warning to those who leave the road.

name: Dol Faeren
description: A Gondorian mountain-town set on a lower foothill of the Dwimorberg overlooking the Morthond Vale. Its stone houses cling to the slope; the people here are farmers and shepherds who work the high pastures above the vale. The shadow of the Dwimorberg — the haunted mountain — gives the place a subdued quality; locals keep to old customs and do not speak lightly of the heights above.

name: Morannon
description: Built by Sauron in the Second Age.. Control of passage into or out of Mordor.. During the Battle of Dagorlad, Sauron was forced to retreat to Barad-dûr when the forces of the Last Alliance broke the defenses here.

name: Annúminas
description: Founded in 3396 by the High-king Elendil.. Royal seat of Arnor; locus of a Palantír; after T.A. 861 the capital of Arthedain.. Annúminas was the home of Arthedain's Kings before the city was sacked in 1409, when the hordes of the Witch-king overran all the former lands of Arnor. Annúminas once held the position of the fairest of Dúnadan cities in the North, with broad, tree-lined avenues and graceful buildings of white marble. No wall surrounded the city, which was deemed a place of peace and learning. Scavengers have picked most of the ruins clean, but tales abound of sealed vaults underneath the library and the palace.

name: Tarmabar
description: Built by the Tarma family in T.A. 2.. A rural power base and summer retreat for the Lords of Tarma.. The Tarmas are second in power only to the King himself, and they have a history of insubordination against the throne.

name: Edhellond
description: Founded by Elves fleeing the Ruin of Beleriand early in the Second Age.. A departure point for Elves leaving Middle-earth.. Edhellond is situated upon a jagged islet at the mouth of the River Morthond.

name: Tir Ethraid
description: Settled by Gondorians, circa T.A. 930.. Maintain watch over the Crossings of Poros; a trading site; a defense against Haradan incursions.. Tir Ethraid is the frequent target of Haradan raids, but King Tarondor has pledged a good deal of gold to strengthen the town's resources.

name: Riavod
description: Settled by Easterlings, circa T.A. 1408.. A trading base for expeditions up the Celduin; a port on the Sea of Rhûn.. Although ruled by a Sagath prince, the Dorwinrim use the town as their main trading base with the lands of Western Endor.

name: Sook Asni
description: Settled by Haradrim, circa T.A. 1400.. Fishing; ship building and repair.. The lacquered tableware created by the craftsmen of Sook Asni is much in demand among the nobility of Umbar.

name: Spathlin
description: Settled by Gondor, circa T.A. 410.. Trading center at the confluence of the Rivers Ciril and Ringló; a collecting point for local farms.. Spathlin controls much of the river traffic to northern Lamedon, but it suffers nonetheless from isolation.

name: Tharagrond
description: Founded by Númenórean colonists c. S.A. 2300.. Defense of the lands of the Principality of Tharagrond; local administration.. Tharagrond is a small and isolated fief, but it carries its autonomy proudly.

name: Pellardur
description: Built by the Captains of Umbar, circa T.A. 750.. Defense of southern land approaches to the city of Umbar; administration of surrounding farmlands.. Pellardur is situated close to the City of Umbar and rests in fairly fertile agricultural lands.
inhabitants: 50% Haradan, 30% Coastal People, 15% Corsair, 5% Black Númenórean

name: Thalion
description: Built by Tarcil of Arnor in T.A. 514.. The seat of House of Dol Tinare.. Thalion is in a sorry state of repair.

name: Argond
description: Finished by the Prince of Dol Calantir in T.A. 887.. A stronghold and administrative center for rural lordship.. Though it appears formidable, the Argond was not designed with defense in mind. Its questionable defensive arrangement has yet to stand the test of a full siege.

name: Annon Baran
description: Settled around T.A. 1200.. Economic hub for western Cardolan; provides fish for region.. Despite its proximity to the Elven land of Harlindon, few of the Cardolani dare cross the river, fearing the spells of the Eldar.

name: Methir
description: Settled by Gondorians, circa T.A. 970.. A trading center in Harondor, supplying and catering to coastal traffic approaching the Ethir Anduin.. Methir was recently sacked by raiding Haradrim, and the town has yet to recover its role as a way-station for river traffic.

name: Little Delving
description: Settled during the migration of Hobbits to the Shire, T.A. 1601-1610.. Agricultural community.. Located on the White Downs, Little Delving has a greater preponderance of smials.

name: Hobbiton/bywater
description: Settled during the migrations of Hobbits to the Shire, T.A. 1601-1610.. An agricultural and pastoral community.. Set on the Great East Road, Hobbiton sees more strange travellers than most of the rural areas of the Shire.

name: Calmirië
description: Settled by Gondorian Dúnedain in the early Third Age, granted charter by Atanatar II of Gondor in T.A. 1220.. An economic focus and market town for the province of Calenardhon; the seat of regional administration.. Calmirië is the largest and most cosmopolitan town of the Gondorian territory of Calenardhon.

name: Calembel
description: Settled by Númenóreans in late Second Age, granted charter by Ostoher of Gondor in T.A. 488.. Provisioning the mining communities in the Ered Nimrais; seat for the Lord Protector of Lamedon.. Calembel is on the road which links central Gondor to the mining communities of the southern White Mountains.

name: Ethring
description: Settled by Gondorians in the late Second Age, granted a royal charter in T.A. 180.. A market town serving most agricultural and mining communities of Lamedon.. Ethring profits from the traffic along the Gondor Road.

name: Glanhir
description: Received royal charter in T.A. 630, possibly constructed upon Second Age Elvish ruins.. A collecting point for local farmers; an economic center for west-central Gondor; a river port.. Situated at the confluence of the Morthond and Ringló Rivers, Glanhir is considered the Gateway to Anfalas.

name: Rendûl
description: Occupied by Gondor since at least T.A. 954, granted a royal charter in T.A. 1187.. Regional trading post on the River Morthond; a defense against Dunnish insurrection.. Rendûl is perhaps the best base for exploration of the interior of Anfalas.

name: Caras Gwindor
description: Settled by Gondorians around T.A. 1098.. Supply station for caravans in Rhovanion and the garrison at Tir Nindor; surveillance of the Dead Marshes.. Caras Gwindor controls most of the trade between Dagorlad and the Anduin, but its isolation limits its size.

name: Barad Harn
description: Founded in T.A. 1256 on the ruins of a previous town destroyed by a hurricane.. Support for the local trade and fishing industry; a locus for Gondorian power in Harondor.. The southernmost outpost of exclusively Gondorian authority. As might be expected, Haradan raids upon this outpost are frequent and brutal. The Governor commits much of his finances to keeping the walls around Barad Harn secure.

name: Dûsalan
description: Settled by entrepreneurs from Umbar in roughly T.A. 307.. A mercantile hub for traffic to and from the South.. Dûsalan is a coastal town ruled by three independent Black Númenórean lords but dominated by Umbar.

name: Kas Marzûk
description: Settled by Gondorians around T.A. 932, occupied by Corsairs in T.A. 1448.. A mercantile center near the mouth of the Harnen; a base for Haradan raids against Gondorian holdings in Harondor.. Kas Marzûk was once a Gondorian town known as Caras Mirilond, but it now rests under the authority of Sangahyandim.

name: Nardol
description: Built by Tarostar of Gondor to replace a wooden signal-platform on the site; completed in T.A. 516.. A link in Gondor's communications network along the Old North Road.. Nardol is the third beacon in the chain.

name: Eolburg
description: Buhr Ailgra is one of the few permanent colonies of the Eothraim, who follow the migratory movements of their flocks across the plains of Rhovanion. The village is a simple community with houses built of straw and peat, surrounded by a sturdy earthen fence.

name: Nornhabar
description: The dwarven population varied considerably, having reached its peak in the Second Age. It was mainly abandoned before the War of the Last Alliance and, although never completely deserted, was largely dilapidated by T.A. 1643.

name: Oatbarton
description: Settled in T.A. 1625 by a family from Bree.. Agriculture.. The rural Hobbits of Oatbarton have a fair amount of contact with Men.

name: Balost
description: Completed in T.A. 923 by Hir Girithlin.. A base for administration of rural estates; a strategic strong point in Gwathló valley between Tharbad and Saralainn.. Balost is a sturdy castle which withstood many attacks from without.

name: Kas Shadoul
description: Original town walls built by Ar-Pharazôn around Second Age 3261, deserted then resettled by Haradrim in the early Third Age.. A mercantile center on the south bank of the Harnen; a gathering point for many tribes of Harad.. Kas Shadoul is a major shipping port, and it is also the source for many of the contraband goods which flow into southern Gondor.

name: Umbar
description: Founded by Tar-Atanamir of Númenor in S.A. 2280, expanded several times.. Capital of Umbar; economic and political center; ship building and repair.. A great monument raised by the Númenórean King Ar-Pharazôn to commemorate his victory over Sauron guards the entrance to the harbor.
inhabitants: 35% Coastal People, 30% Corsair, 25% Haradan, 10% Black Númenórean

name: Hwarin
description: A small coastal village in Rast Rhaen, a sheltered garth on the central Andrast coast. Its few stone houses and fishing boats sit in a protected cove; the people are Dunir fisher-folk who live by the sea and avoid the attention of passing ships.

name: Kas Shafras
description: Settled by Haradrim around T.A. 800.. A trading port, roughly halfway between the Ethir Anduin and the City of Umbar.. Kas Shafras is renowned for the rare medicinal herbs grown in its environs.

name: Bree
description: Settled by migrating Dunmen in the early Second Age.. Collecting point for the products of surrounding farms; trading center due to its location at the junction of the Greenway and the Great East Road.. Bree is the largest town in Bree-land, a region of Arthedain that is peaceful, agricultural, and largely autonomous. The town is built around a large hill and is protected by a formidable dike surmounted by a thick hedge. Numerous smials, or Hobbit-holes, can be found in the flanks of Bree-hill. Both Men and Hobbits live in Bree, making it one of the few locations in Middle-earth where co-operation between folk of different races occurs. Due to its central location, Bree witnesses many strange folk passing through its streets, and the town is a perfect place to acquire or sell information.

name: Caras Celairnen
description: First settled around T.A. 740.. A regional market town in southwestern Arthedain.. Caras Celairnen is the westernmost center of purely Dúnadan culture in the North.

name: Ilanin
description: Traditional trading site since at least T.A. 950.. A site for commerce; a gathering spot for many Easterling tribes.. Ilanin has few permanent buildings. Most structures are simple tents which disappear as soon as a market ends.

name: Larach Duhnnan
description: Established as an outpost on the Old North Road, T.A. 3; taken over by Dunlendings, T.A. 275.. An urban center for the scattered Dunnish tribes; a neutral place of contact for negotiation.. Larach Duhnnan is in the territory claimed by Clan Feargan, but the foreign traffic passing through it makes it an excellent place for neutral discussion and trade.

name: Minas Ongoth
description: Ruined city.

name: Rood
description: Grew around Baranduin river traffic in early Third Age.. A collecting point for area farms; a shipping station for freight on Baranduin.. With the sack of Annúminas, Rood began to fall into a decline, becoming a den for thieves and bandits.

name: Gamwich
description: Settled during the migration of Hobbits into the Shire, T.A. 1601-1610.. An agricultural community.. Gamwich is the Westron translation of the Kuduk name Galabas.

name: Sackville
description: Settled during the migration to the Shire, 1601-1610.. Agriculture.. Proximity to the rugged Men of Eriador's Red Hills bestows a reputation for crudity upon the residents of Sackville.

name: Metraith
description: Founded by Tarcil of Arnor as a summer residence, T.A. 514.. Seat of the Lords of Dol Tinare; a supply center and minor market for traffic on the Greenway.. The Princes of Tinare inhabit a large and crumbling mansion in Metraith.

name: Minas Dûrlith
description: Crafted by Sauron sometime during the Second Age.. Control of the plain known as Gaer Dûrlith.. Minas Dûrlith is one of the few Sauronic strongholds to survive the invasion of Mordor by the Last Alliance of Elves and Men.

name: Buhr Fram
description: Name given to Wraecaburg after the Éothraim settled there in the nineteenth century of the Third Age.. Settlement of Éothraim.. Name given to Wraecaburg after the Éothraim settled there in the nineteenth century of the Third Age.

name: Ardûmir
description: Built by Númenórean colonists, circa S.A. 3290.. A rural stronghold for a Captain of Umbar; administration of the surrounding lands; guards entry into the Bay of Umbar.. The castle's eastern curtain wall drops directly into the lake that partially surrounds Ardûmir.
inhabitants: 40% Coastal People, 30% Haradan, 25% Corsair, 5% Black Númenórean

name: Angsûl
description: Completed by the Witch-king in T.A. 1349.. Control of traffic into and out of Angmar.. The Witch-king keeps a large garrison stationed in Angsûl to watch for spies attempting to penetrate Angmar's central plateau.

name: Hornburg
description: Built soon after the War of the Last Alliance at the end of the Second Age.. Quarters for a large portion of Gondor's army in Calenardhon; administration and protection of the lands from the Isen Fords to Calmirië.. 'Hornburg' is a Westron name; most Gondorians refer to the citadel as Ostiras.

name: Angsûl
description: Completed by the Witch-king in T.A. 1349.. Control of traffic into and out of Angmar.. The Witch-king keeps a large garrison stationed in Angsûl to watch for spies attempting to penetrate Angmar's central plateau.

name: Ardûmir
description: Built by Númenórean colonists, circa S.A. 3290.. A rural stronghold for a Captain of Umbar; administration of the surrounding lands; guards entry into the Bay of Umbar.. The castle's eastern curtain wall drops directly into the lake that partially surrounds Ardûmir.
inhabitants: 40% Coastal People, 30% Haradan, 25% Corsair, 5% Black Númenórean

name: Caldur
description: Raised by the Captains of Umbar c. T.A. 520.. A base for sorties against Gondor; administration of the lands around the northern entrance to the Bay of Umbar.. Caldûr is located on a steep outcropping on the northern penninsula guarding the Nen Umbar.
inhabitants: 50% Coastal People, 20% Corsair, 20% Haradan, 10% Black Númenórean

name: Thorontir
description: Founded by Gondor, circa T.A. 500.. A center for regional administration; a garrison for Gondor's Eastern Army.. The fortress is located just off the Men Romen, several miles east of the town of Thorontir.

name: Halifirien
description: Current tower completed in T.A. 529.. A link in Gondor's chain of communications in Anórien.. Halifirien rests on the border of Anórien and Calenardhon.

name: Calenhad
description: Ordered built in T.A. 491 by Gondor's King on the site of an earlier signal-platform.. Communications in northern Gondor.. Calenhad is the sixth beacon in the chain stretching from Minas Anor to the northwest.

name: Min-rimmon
description: Current tower completed in T.A. 516.. A link in Gondor's northern communications network.. The fifth beacon in the series was built on a mountain named Rimmon.

name: Eilenach
description: Current tower completed in T.A. 522.. A link in Gondor's chain of communication with the North.. Eilenach is located on the tallest and steepest hill within Druadan Forest.

name: Erelas
description: Construction begun on the site of an older, simpler signal-platform in T.A. 494.. A link in Gondor's northern chain of communication.. Erelas is the fourth beacon in the series running northwest from Minas Anor.

name: Amrelor
description: Ruined city.

name: Linhir
description: Founded by Anárion in S.A. 3421.. A port and center for trade and manufacturing, situated at the point where the Gondor Road crosses the Gilrain.. Linhir is prosperous and busy, and a large population of unseemly characters has grown to prey upon the weak and gullible.

name: Imladris
description: Founded by Elrond Half-elven in S.A. 1697.. A haven for healing, learning, and the arts.. The house of Elrond Half-elven is located in a narrow ravine, not easily found in the broken lands of fallen Rhudaur.

name: Tharbad
description: A Númenórean river port dating to the mid-Second Age.. A major urban hub; the effective administrative center of Cardolan; an outpost of Gondorian authority.. Since the fall of King Ostoher in T.A. 1410, the former Númenórean port of Tharbad has declined from the glory it held in ages past.
inhabitants: 60% Cardolani, 20% Dunnish, 10% Northman, 5% Eriadoran, 5% Other Mannish

name: Harlond
description: Settled soon after founding of Minas Anor, circa S.A. 3320.. The port for Minas Anor; oversees river traffic on Anduin.. Harlond is a busy town, handling almost all of the freight from southern Gondor bound for the lands of Calenardhon and Rhovanion.

name: Buhr Ailgra
description: Founded by Éothraim, circa T.A. 1380.. Defense against Easterling attacks, a trading center for Eothraim.. Buhr Ailgra is one of the few permanent settlements of the Eothraim, who follow the migrations of their herds through the plains of Rhovanion. The town is a simple settlement of thatch and turf houses surrounded by a stout earthen rampart.

name: Barak-shathur
description: Founded by settlers from Azanulinbar-dûm c. T.A. 1490.. Exploitation of the mineral resources of the Iron Hills.. The Khuzdul name of the stronghold is thought to mean 'Axe Mountain' in the Westron.

name: Tir Anduin
description: Settled by Gondorians, circa T.A. 1402; granted royal charter in T.A. 1560.. Northernmost significant Gondorian trading outpost; location of a ferry across the Anduin.. The ferry of Tir Anduin and the nearby ford at the Undeeps are the safest crossings of the Anduin between Osgiliath and the Old Ford.

name: Tir Nindor
description: Constructed by Gondor around T.A. 410.. Guarding the only safe and dry passage through the Dead Marshes.. Tir Nindor is a strategic castle guarding the passage through the Dead Marshes.

name: Tuckburrow
description: Settled by Hobbits during the migration to the Shire, T.A. 1601-1610.. Clan dwelling; agriculture.. The village is located in the Green-hill country of the Westfarthing of the Shire.

name: Amon Dîn
description: Construction of a signal-platform ordered in S.A. 3325 by Anárion of Gondor. Upgraded in the fifth century of the Third Age.. Part of Gondor's northern communications network.. Amon Dîn was the first, counting from east to west, in a chain of seven beacon-towers. Its watch-fire relayed warning to Minas Anor of any attack on Gondor's northern frontiers.

name: Bar-Lymen
description: A self-governing township and Grithion of Lebennin on the middle Gilrain, its name meaning 'Home of the Blue Way'. The settlement clusters around a ford or bridge over the Gilrain; stone houses and a small market serve the surrounding farmland. Its autonomy as a Grithion gives it a slightly more independent character than the usual Gondorian village.

name: Bridge Of Stonebows
description: Built by Arthedain, T.A. 1102.. Passage across the River Baranduin on the Great East Road.. The King of Arthedain grants the Hobbits the right to collect a toll, which they keep at a modest few copper coins.

name: Cameth Brin
description: Captured and enhanced by Dúnedain, T.A. 162.. A stable center of administration for Rhudaur; domination of local Hillman tribes.. Currently, Cameth Brin serves as the seat of the Hillman King Broggha.

name: Minas Ithil
description: Founded by Isildur in S.A. 3320, rebuilt in T.A. 1.. Control of the passes into Mordor; garrison for Gondor's army in Ithilien; location of a Palantír.. Minas Ithil is a Gondorian city protected by a deep valley in the Ephel Dúath.

name: Elostirion
description: Built by Gil-galad as a gift to Elendil.. Home to one of the Palantíri.. Elostirion is the tallest and westernmost of the three White Towers in the Emyn Beraid.

name: Bridge Of Stonebows
description: Built by Arthedain, T.A. 1102.. Passage across the River Baranduin on the Great East Road.. The King of Arthedain grants the Hobbits the right to collect a toll, which they keep at a modest few copper coins.

name: Bridge Of Mitheithel
description: Built by Arnor, T.A. 320.. Passage across the Mitheithel on the Great East Road. The Last Bridge, so-named because it is the farthest east on the Road, was built of stone with three great arches.

name: Belegost
description: The First Age, possibly before the Sun and the Moon first rose.. Exploit the riches of the Ered Luin, defense against all enemies.. A few bold Dwarven explorers have recently begun to explore the ruins in hopes of finding the artifacts of their ancestors.

name: Crossroads Of The
description: First mentioned in the time of King Anárion.. A landmark for travellers in Ithilien.. The Crossroads are the junction of the road between Osgiliath and Minas Ithil with the Morannon-Harad Road.

name: Eithel Celebrin
description: Settled by trading company from Pelargir, circa T.A. 1290.. Reprovisioning caravans near the eastern rim of the Dead Marshes.. Eithel Celebrin suffers from the growing tendency of merchants bound for Rhovanion to ship their goods by means of the safer route up the Anduin.

name: Fennas Drúnin
description: First settled by Númenóreans from Tharbad around S.A. 2910.. A social and economic focal point for the farms and villages of Feotar.. A Council runs most affairs in the town, which relies upon the local farms and vineyards for its prosperity.

name: The Forsaken Inn
description: In business since T.A. 1412.. Reprovisioning and boarding of travellers on the Great East Road at the Mitheithel Bridge.. Operated by a family of Dunlendings, the Forsaken Inn is one of the few safe and neutral places an innocent traveller may rest on the road from Rivendell to Bree.

name: Nogothiant
description: Built by the Naugrim of Moria before S.A. 1693.. Passage across the Anduin on the Great East Road.. The Nogothiant is crumbling, having lasted without maintenance for many centuries.

name: Staddle
description: Settled by Bree-land farmers, circa T.A. 1250.. Agriculture.. Staddle is just a short walk from Bree, and it relies upon its larger neighbor for most of the services a small agrarian community cannot provide for itself.

name: Strayhold
description: Settled by Gramuz outcasts, circa T.A. 1340.. Trade with Gramuz and Gondor; a local economic focal point.. Strayhold rests near the eastern edge of Mirkwood, situated in a deep gorge near the edge of the Talath Harroch.

name: Sudúri
description: Founded by the adventurer Gordaigh in T.A. 1503 on site of a Dúnadan fortress.. Administrative center for the Kingdom of Saralainn; a meeting place for Dunnish tribes.. The town was originally built by Númenóreans, but now almost all of the inhabitants are Dunnish in origin.

name: Combe
description: Settled by farmers from Bree in T.A. 1415.. An economic center for those unwilling to trek to Bree; agriculture.. Combe is the most isolated of Bree-land's villages.

name: Derwath
description: Settled by Gondorian pioneers around T.A. 609.. An agricultural community; serves administrative roles in the sparsely populated Westmarch.. The village serves as headquarters to a detachment of rangers.

name: Saarkaana
description: Possibly Númenórean (?).. Unknown, possibly ceremonial.. Saarkaana is an Umbarean fortress in the parched Talath Naur, situated near the ruins of a Second Age structure.

name: Erebor
description: Natural formation, enlarged by Dwarves in Second Age (?).. Unknown.. Many tunnels and caverns lie underneath the Lonely Mountain near the town of Dale in northern Rhovanion.

name: Nogrod
description: First delved in the Early First Age.. The mining, smelting, and working of precious metals found in the Ered Luin.. Nogrod was destroyed in the cataclysm that sank Beleriand beneath the ocean.

name: Woodmen-town
description: Settled permanently around T.A. 1580.. A gathering place for the Woodman clans; the home of the Althyn; the site of an annual fair.. Woodmen-town is built upon great platforms in the trees in the Elvish manner, for the Woodmen are on friendly terms with the Elves of the Forest.

name: Wraecaburg
description: Settled by Northmen around T.A. 1000.. Agriculture; a center for trade in the northern Anduin Vales.. Though not directly under the sway of the Witch-king, Wraecaburg suffers from frequent raids, despair, and a large population of shady characters from beyond Rhovanion.

name: Warfinger
description: Built by the Gondorian Eastern Army around T.A. 1428.. A waystation along the south fork of the Men Romen; a defensive point against invasion from the East.. The small garrison at Warfinger is situated far away from the authority of the Regent in Thorontir.
